  def secure_params(secure, reason, source = "unknown")
    {
      secure: secure,
      security_last_changed_reason: reason + " | source: #{source}",
      security_last_changed_at: Time.zone.now,
    }
  end

  def self.migrate_to_new_scheme(limit: nil)
    problems = []

    DistributedMutex.synchronize("migrate_upload_to_new_scheme") do
      if SiteSetting.migrate_to_new_scheme
        max_file_size_kb = [
          SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb,
          SiteSetting.max_attachment_size_kb,
        ].max.kilobytes

        local_store = FileStore::LocalStore.new
        db = RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.current_db

        scope =
          Upload
            .by_users
            .where("url NOT LIKE '%/original/_X/%' AND url LIKE ?", "%/uploads/#{db}%")
            .order(id: :desc)

        scope = scope.limit(limit) if limit

        if scope.count == 0
          SiteSetting.migrate_to_new_scheme = false
          return problems
        end

        remap_scope = nil

        scope.each do |upload|
          begin
            # keep track of the url
            previous_url = upload.url.dup
            # where is the file currently stored?
            external = previous_url =~ %r{\A//}
            # download if external
            if external
              url = SiteSetting.scheme + ":" + previous_url

              begin
                retries ||= 0

                file =
                  FileHelper.download(
                    url,
                    max_file_size: max_file_size_kb,
                    tmp_file_name: "discourse",
                    follow_redirect: true,
                  )
              rescue OpenURI::HTTPError
                retry if (retries += 1) < 1
                next
              end

              path = file.path
            else
              path = local_store.path_for(upload)
            end
            # compute SHA if missing
            upload.sha1 = Upload.generate_digest(path) if upload.sha1.blank?

            # store to new location & update the filesize
            File.open(path) do |f|
              upload.url = Discourse.store.store_upload(f, upload)
              upload.filesize = f.size
              upload.save!(validate: false)
            end
            # remap the URLs
            DbHelper.remap(UrlHelper.absolute(previous_url), upload.url) unless external

            DbHelper.remap(
              previous_url,
              upload.url,
              excluded_tables: %w[
                posts
                post_search_data
                incoming_emails
                notifications
                single_sign_on_records
                stylesheet_cache
                topic_search_data
                users
                user_emails
                draft_sequences
                optimized_images
              ],
            )

            remap_scope ||=
              begin
                Post
                  .with_deleted
                  .where(
                    "raw ~ '/uploads/#{db}/\\d+/' OR raw ~ '/uploads/#{db}/original/(\\d|[a-z])/'",
                  )
                  .select(:id, :raw, :cooked)
                  .all
              end

            remap_scope.each do |post|
              post.raw.gsub!(previous_url, upload.url)
              post.cooked.gsub!(previous_url, upload.url)
              if post.changed?
                Post.with_deleted.where(id: post.id).update_all(raw: post.raw, cooked: post.cooked)
              end
            end

            upload.optimized_images.find_each(&:destroy!)
            upload.rebake_posts_on_old_scheme
            # remove the old file (when local)
            FileUtils.rm(path, force: true) unless external
          rescue => e
            problems << { upload: upload, ex: e }
          ensure
            file&.unlink
            file&.close
          end
        end
      end
    end

    problems
  end

  def self.extract_upload_ids(raw)
    return [] if raw.blank?

    sha1s = []

    raw.scan(/\/(\h{40})/).each { |match| sha1s << match[0] }

    raw
      .scan(%r{/([a-zA-Z0-9]+)})
      .each { |match| sha1s << Upload.sha1_from_base62_encoded(match[0]) }

    Upload.where(sha1: sha1s.uniq).pluck(:id)
  end

  def self.backfill_dominant_colors!(count)
    Upload
      .where(dominant_color: nil)
      .order("id desc")
      .first(count)
      .each { |upload| upload.calculate_dominant_color! }
  end

  private

  def short_url_basename
    "#{Upload.base62_sha1(sha1)}#{extension.present? ? ".#{extension}" : ""}"
  end

  def should_convert_video?
    !skip_video_conversion && SiteSetting.video_conversion_enabled &&
      SiteSetting.Upload.enable_s3_uploads && FileHelper.is_supported_video?(original_filename) &&
      !OptimizedVideo.exists?(upload_id: id)
  end

  def enqueue_video_conversion_job
    Jobs.enqueue(:convert_video, upload_id: id)
  end
end

# == Schema Information
#
# Table name: uploads
#
#  id                           :integer          not null, primary key
#  user_id                      :integer          not null
#  original_filename            :string           not null
#  filesize                     :bigint           not null
#  width                        :integer
#  height                       :integer
#  url                          :string           not null
#  created_at                   :datetime         not null
#  updated_at                   :datetime         not null
#  sha1                         :string(40)
#  origin                       :string(2000)
#  retain_hours                 :integer
#  extension                    :string(10)
#  thumbnail_width              :integer
#  thumbnail_height             :integer
#  etag                         :string
#  secure                       :boolean          default(FALSE), not null
#  access_control_post_id       :bigint
#  original_sha1                :string
#  animated                     :boolean
#  verification_status          :integer          default(1), not null
#  security_last_changed_at     :datetime

def self.consider_for_reuse(upload, post)
    return upload if !SiteSetting.secure_uploads? || upload.blank? || post.blank?
    if !upload.matching_access_control_post?(post) || upload.uploaded_before_secure_uploads_enabled?
      return nil
    end
    upload
  end

  def self.secure_uploads_url?(url)
    # we do not want to exclude topic links that for whatever reason
    # have secure-uploads in the URL e.g. /t/secure-uploads-are-cool/223452
    route = UrlHelper.rails_route_from_url(url)
    return false if route.blank?
    route[:action] == "show_secure" && route[:controller] == "uploads" &&
      FileHelper.is_supported_media?(url)
  rescue ActionController::RoutingError
    false
  end

  def self.signed_url_from_secure_uploads_url(url, include_content_disposition:)
    route = UrlHelper.rails_route_from_url(url)
    url = Rails.application.routes.url_for(route.merge(only_path: true))
    secure_upload_s3_path = url[url.index(route[:path])..-1]
    Discourse.store.signed_url_for_path(secure_upload_s3_path, include_content_disposition:)
  end

  def self.secure_uploads_url_from_upload_url(url)
    return url if !url.include?(SiteSetting.Upload.absolute_base_url)
    uri = URI.parse(url)
    Rails.application.routes.url_for(
      controller: "uploads",
      action: "show_secure",
      path: uri.path[1..-1],
      only_path: true,
    )
  end

  def self.short_path(sha1:, extension:)
    @url_helpers ||= Rails.application.routes.url_helpers

    @url_helpers.upload_short_path(base62: self.base62_sha1(sha1), extension: extension)
  end

  def self.base62_sha1(sha1)
    Base62.encode(sha1.hex)
  end

  def base62_sha1
    Upload.base62_sha1(self.sha1)
  end

  def local?
    !(url =~ %r{\A(https?:)?//})
  end

  def fix_dimensions!
    return if !FileHelper.is_supported_image?("image.#{extension}")

    begin
      path =
        if local?
          Discourse.store.path_for(self)
        else
          Discourse.store.download!(self)
        end

      if extension == "svg"
        w, h =
          begin
            Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
              "identify",
              "-ping",
              "-format",
              "%w %h",
              path,
              timeout: MAX_IDENTIFY_SECONDS,
            ).split(" ")
          rescue StandardError
            [0, 0]
          end
      else
        w, h = FastImage.new(path, raise_on_failure: true).size
      end

      self.width = w || 0
      self.height = h || 0

      self.thumbnail_width, self.thumbnail_height = ImageSizer.resize(w, h)

      self.update_columns(
        width: width,
        height: height,
        thumbnail_width: thumbnail_width,
        thumbnail_height: thumbnail_height,
      )
    rescue => e
      Discourse.warn_exception(e, message: "Error getting image dimensions")
    end
    nil
  end

  # on demand image size calculation, this allows us to null out image sizes
  # and still handle as needed
  def get_dimension(key)
    if v = self[key]
      return v
    end
    fix_dimensions!
    self[key]
  end

  def width
    get_dimension(:width)
  end

  def height
    get_dimension(:height)
  end

  def thumbnail_width
    get_dimension(:thumbnail_width)
  end

  def thumbnail_height
    get_dimension(:thumbnail_height)
  end

  def dominant_color(calculate_if_missing: false)
    val = read_attribute(:dominant_color)
    if val.nil? && calculate_if_missing
      calculate_dominant_color!
      read_attribute(:dominant_color)
    else
      val
    end
  end

  def calculate_dominant_color!(local_path = nil)
    color = nil

    color = "" if !FileHelper.is_supported_image?("image.#{extension}") || extension == "svg"

    if color.nil?
      local_path ||=
        if local?
          Discourse.store.path_for(self)
        else
          Discourse.store.download(self)
        end

      if local_path.nil?
        # Download failed. Could be too large to download, or file could be missing in s3
        color = ""
      end

      color ||=
        begin
          data =
            Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
              "nice",
              "-n",
              "10",
              "convert",
              local_path,
              "-depth",
              "8",
              "-resize",
              "1x1",
              "-define",
              "histogram:unique-colors=true",
              "-format",
              "%c",
              "histogram:info:",
              timeout: DOMINANT_COLOR_COMMAND_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
            )

          # Output format:
          # 1: (110.873,116.226,93.8821) #6F745E srgb(43.4798%,45.5789%,36.8165%)

          color = data[/#([0-9A-F]{6})/, 1]

          raise "Calculated dominant color but unable to parse output:\n#{data}" if color.nil?

          color
        rescue Discourse::Utils::CommandError
          # Timeout or unable to parse image
          # This can happen due to bad user input - ignore and save
          # an empty string to prevent re-evaluation
          ""
        end
    end

    if persisted?
      self.update_column(:dominant_color, color)
    else
      self.dominant_color = color
    end
  end

  def target_image_quality(local_path, test_quality)
    @file_quality ||=
      begin
        Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
          "identify",
          "-ping",
          "-format",
          "%Q",
          local_path,
          timeout: MAX_IDENTIFY_SECONDS,
        ).to_i
      rescue StandardError
        0
      end

    test_quality if @file_quality == 0 || @file_quality > test_quality
  end

  def self.sha1_from_short_path(path)
    self.sha1_from_base62_encoded($2) if path =~ %r{(/uploads/short-url/)([a-zA-Z0-9]+)(\..*)?}
  end

  def self.sha1_from_short_url(url)
    self.sha1_from_base62_encoded($2) if url =~ %r{(upload://)?([a-zA-Z0-9]+)(\..*)?}
  end

  def self.sha1_from_long_url(url)
    $2 if url =~ URL_REGEX || url =~ OptimizedImage::URL_REGEX
  end

  def self.sha1_from_base62_encoded(encoded_sha1)
    return nil if encoded_sha1.length > MAX_BASE62_SHA1_LENGTH
    sha1 = Base62.decode(encoded_sha1).to_s(16)

    if sha1.length > SHA1_LENGTH
      nil
    else
      sha1.rjust(SHA1_LENGTH, "0")
    end
  end

  def self.generate_digest(path)
    Digest::SHA1.file(path).hexdigest
  end

  def human_filesize
    number_to_human_size(self.filesize)
  end

  def rebake_posts_on_old_scheme
    self.posts.where("cooked LIKE '%/_optimized/%'").find_each(&:rebake!)
  end

  def update_secure_status(source: "unknown", override: nil)
    if override.nil?
      mark_secure, reason = UploadSecurity.new(self).should_be_secure_with_reason
    else
      mark_secure = override
      reason = "manually overridden"
    end

    secure_status_did_change = self.secure? != mark_secure
    self.update(secure_params(mark_secure, reason, source))

    if secure_status_did_change && Discourse.store.external?
      Discourse.store.update_upload_access_control(self)
      sync_optimized_videos_secure_status(mark_secure)
    end

    secure_status_did_change
  end

  def sync_optimized_videos_secure_status(mark_secure)
    optimized_videos
      .includes(:optimized_upload)
      .each do |optimized_video|
        optimized_upload = optimized_video.optimized_upload
        if optimized_upload.secure? != mark_secure
          optimized_upload.update_secure_status(
            source: "sync_with_original_upload",
            override: mark_secure,
          )

# frozen_string_literal: true

require "digest/sha1"

class Upload < ActiveRecord::Base
  include ActionView::Helpers::NumberHelper
  include HasUrl

  SHA1_LENGTH = 40
  SEEDED_ID_THRESHOLD = 0
  URL_REGEX = %r{(/original/\dX[/\.\w]*/(\h+)[\.\w]*)}
  MAX_IDENTIFY_SECONDS = 5
  DOMINANT_COLOR_COMMAND_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 5
  # the maximum length of a base62 encoded sha1
  MAX_BASE62_SHA1_LENGTH = 27

  belongs_to :user
  belongs_to :access_control_post, class_name: "Post"

  # when we access this post we don't care if the post
  # is deleted
  def access_control_post
    Post.unscoped { super }
  end

  has_many :post_hotlinked_media, dependent: :destroy, class_name: "PostHotlinkedMedia"
  has_many :optimized_images, dependent: :destroy
  has_many :optimized_videos, dependent: :destroy
  has_many :optimized_video_uploads, through: :optimized_videos, source: :optimized_upload
  has_many :user_uploads, dependent: :destroy
  has_many :upload_references, dependent: :destroy
  has_many :posts, through: :upload_references, source: :target, source_type: "Post"
  has_many :topic_thumbnails
  has_many :badges, foreign_key: :image_upload_id, dependent: :nullify
  after_create :enqueue_video_conversion_job, if: :should_convert_video?

  attr_accessor :for_group_message
  attr_accessor :for_theme
  attr_accessor :for_private_message
  attr_accessor :for_export
  attr_accessor :for_site_setting
  attr_accessor :for_gravatar
  attr_accessor :site_setting_name
  attr_accessor :validate_file_size
  attr_accessor :skip_video_conversion

  validates :filesize, presence: true
  validates :original_filename, presence: true
  validates :dominant_color, length: { is: 6 }, allow_blank: true

  validates_with UploadValidator

  before_destroy do
    UserProfile.where(card_background_upload_id: self.id).update_all(card_background_upload_id: nil)
    UserProfile.where(profile_background_upload_id: self.id).update_all(
      profile_background_upload_id: nil,
    )
  end

  after_destroy do
    User.where(uploaded_avatar_id: self.id).update_all(uploaded_avatar_id: nil)
    UserAvatar.where(gravatar_upload_id: self.id).update_all(gravatar_upload_id: nil)
    UserAvatar.where(custom_upload_id: self.id).update_all(custom_upload_id: nil)
  end

  scope :by_users, -> { where("uploads.id > ?", SEEDED_ID_THRESHOLD) }

  scope :without_s3_file_missing_confirmed_verification_status,
        -> do
          where.not(verification_status: Upload.verification_statuses[:s3_file_missing_confirmed])
        end

  scope :with_invalid_etag_verification_status,
        -> { where(verification_status: Upload.verification_statuses[:invalid_etag]) }

  def self.verification_statuses
    @verification_statuses ||=
      Enum.new(
        unchecked: 1,
        verified: 2,
        invalid_etag: 3, # Used by S3Inventory to mark S3 Upload records that have an invalid ETag value compared to the ETag value of the inventory file
        s3_file_missing_confirmed: 4, # Used by S3Inventory to skip S3 Upload records that are confirmed to not be backed by a file in the S3 file store
      )
  end

  def self.fetch_from(sha1:, url:)
    sha1.presence.try { |_| find_by(sha1:) } || get_from_url(url)
  end

  def self.mark_invalid_s3_uploads_as_missing
    Upload.with_invalid_etag_verification_status.update_all(
      verification_status: Upload.verification_statuses[:s3_file_missing_confirmed],
    )
  end

  def self.add_unused_callback(&block)
    (@unused_callbacks ||= []) << block
  end

  def self.unused_callbacks
    @unused_callbacks
  end

  def self.reset_unused_callbacks
    @unused_callbacks = []
  end

  def self.add_in_use_callback(&block)
    (@in_use_callbacks ||= []) << block
  end

  def self.in_use_callbacks
    @in_use_callbacks
  end

  def self.reset_in_use_callbacks
    @in_use_callbacks = []
  end

  def self.with_no_non_post_relations
    self.joins(
      "LEFT JOIN upload_references ur ON ur.upload_id = uploads.id AND ur.target_type != 'Post'",
    ).where("ur.upload_id IS NULL")
  end

  def initialize(*args)
    super
    self.validate_file_size = true
  end

  def to_s
    self.url
  end

  def to_markdown
    UploadMarkdown.new(self).to_markdown
  end

  def thumbnail(width = self.thumbnail_width, height = self.thumbnail_height)
    optimized_images.find_by(width: width, height: height)
  end

  def has_thumbnail?(width, height)
    thumbnail(width, height).present?
  end

  def create_thumbnail!(width, height, opts = nil)
    return unless SiteSetting.create_thumbnails?
    opts ||= {}

    save(validate: false) if get_optimized_image(width, height, opts)
  end

  # this method attempts to correct old incorrect extensions
  def get_optimized_image(width, height, opts = nil)
    opts ||= {}

    fix_image_extension if (!extension || extension.length == 0)

    opts = opts.merge(raise_on_error: true)
    begin
      OptimizedImage.create_for(self, width, height, opts)
    rescue => ex
      Rails.logger.info ex if Rails.env.development?
      opts = opts.merge(raise_on_error: false)
      if fix_image_extension
        OptimizedImage.create_for(self, width, height, opts)
      else
        nil
      end
    end
  end

  def content
    original_path = Discourse.store.path_for(self)

    if original_path.blank?
      File.read(Discourse.store.download!(self))
    else
      File.read(original_path)
    end
  end

  def fix_image_extension
    return false if extension == "unknown"

    begin
      # this is relatively cheap once cached
      original_path = Discourse.store.path_for(self)
      original_path = Discourse.store.download(self) if original_path.blank?

      image_info =
        begin
          image = FastImage.new(original_path)
          image.type # eager load to rescue errors early
          image
        rescue StandardError
          nil
        end
      new_extension = image_info&.type&.to_s || "unknown"

      if new_extension != self.extension
        self.update_columns(extension: new_extension)
        true
      end
    rescue StandardError
      self.update_columns(extension: "unknown")
      true
    end
  end

  def destroy
    Upload.transaction do
      Discourse.store.remove_upload(self)
      super
    end
  end

  def short_url
    "upload://#{short_url_basename}"
  end

  def uploaded_before_secure_uploads_enabled?
    original_sha1.blank?
  end

  def matching_access_control_post?(post)
    access_control_post_id == post.id
  end

  def copied_from_other_post?(post)
    return false if access_control_post_id.blank?
    !matching_access_control_post?(post)
  end

  def short_path
    self.class.short_path(sha1: self.sha1, extension: self.extension)
  end

  def self.consider_for_reuse(upload, post)

module HasUrl
  extend ActiveSupport::Concern

  class_methods do
    def extract_url(url)
      url.match(self::URL_REGEX)
    end

    def extract_sha1(path)
      data = extract_url(path)
      return if data.blank?

      sha1 = data[2]
      return if sha1&.length != Upload::SHA1_LENGTH

      sha1
    end

    def get_from_url(url)
      return if url.blank?

      uri =
        begin
          URI(UrlHelper.unencode(url))
        rescue URI::Error
        end

      return if uri&.path.blank?
      data = extract_url(uri.path)

      if data.blank?
        result = nil
        result ||= self.find_by(url: uri.path)
        return result
      end

      result = nil

      if self.name == "Upload"
        sha1 = data[2]
        result = self.find_by(sha1: sha1) if sha1&.length == Upload::SHA1_LENGTH
      end

      result || self.find_by("url LIKE ?", "%#{data[1]}")
    end

    def get_from_urls(upload_urls)
      urls = []
      sha1s = []

      upload_urls.each do |url|
        next if url.blank?

        uri =
          begin
            URI(UrlHelper.unencode(url))
          rescue URI::Error
          end

        next if uri&.path.blank?
        urls << uri.path

        if data = extract_url(uri.path).presence
          urls << data[1]
          sha1s << data[2] if self.name == "Upload"
        end
      end

      self.where(url: urls).or(self.where(sha1: sha1s))
    end
  end
end

   105	  def transform!
   106	    raise CannotPromoteError if !can_promote?
   107	    external_upload_stub.update!(status: ExternalUploadStub.statuses[:uploaded])
   108	
   109	    # We require that the file size is specified ahead of time, and compare
   110	    # it here to make sure that people are not uploading excessively large
   111	    # files to the external provider. If this happens, the user will be banned
   112	    # from uploading to the external provider for N minutes.
   113	    if external_size != external_upload_stub.filesize
   114	      ExternalUploadManager.ban_user_from_external_uploads!(
   115	        user: external_upload_stub.created_by,
   116	        ban_minutes: SIZE_MISMATCH_BAN_MINUTES,
   117	      )
   118	      raise SizeMismatchError.new(
   119	              "expected: #{external_upload_stub.filesize}, actual: #{external_size}",
   120	            )
   121	    end
   122	
   123	    if UPLOAD_TYPES_EXCLUDED_FROM_UPLOAD_PROMOTION.include?(external_upload_stub.upload_type)
   124	      move_to_final_destination
   125	    else
   126	      promote_to_upload
   127	    end
   128	  rescue StandardError
   129	    if !SiteSetting.enable_upload_debug_mode
   130	      # We don't need to do anything special to abort multipart uploads here,
   131	      # because at this point (calling promote_to_upload!), the multipart
   132	      # upload would already be complete.
   133	      @store.delete_file(external_upload_stub.key)
   134	      external_upload_stub.destroy!
   135	    else
   136	      external_upload_stub.update(status: ExternalUploadStub.statuses[:failed])
   137	    end
   138	
   139	    raise
   140	  end
   141	
   142	  private
   143	
   144	  def promote_to_upload
   145	    # This could be legitimately nil, if it's too big to download on the
   146	    # server, or it could have failed. To this end we set a should_download
   147	    # variable as well to check.
   148	    tempfile = nil
   149	    should_download = external_size < DOWNLOAD_LIMIT
   150	
   151	    if should_download
   152	      tempfile = download(external_upload_stub.key, external_upload_stub.upload_type)
   153	
   154	      raise DownloadFailedError if tempfile.blank?
   155	
   156	      actual_sha1 = Upload.generate_digest(tempfile)
   157	      raise ChecksumMismatchError if external_sha1 && external_sha1 != actual_sha1
   158	    end
   159	
   160	    opts = {
   161	      type: external_upload_stub.upload_type,
   162	      existing_external_upload_key: external_upload_stub.key,
   163	      external_upload_too_big: external_size > DOWNLOAD_LIMIT,
   164	      filesize: external_size,
   165	    }.merge(@upload_create_opts)
   166	
   167	    UploadCreator.new(tempfile, external_upload_stub.original_filename, opts).create_for(
   168	      external_upload_stub.created_by_id,
   169	    )
   170	  ensure
   171	    tempfile&.close!
   172	  end
   173	
   174	  def move_to_final_destination
   175	    content_type =
   176	      MiniMime.lookup_by_filename(external_upload_stub.original_filename)&.content_type ||
   177	        "application/gzip"
   178	    @store.move_existing_stored_upload(
   179	      existing_external_upload_key: external_upload_stub.key,
   180	      original_filename: external_upload_stub.original_filename,
   181	      content_type: content_type,
   182	    )
   183	    Struct.new(:errors).new([])
   184	  end
   185	
   186	  def external_stub_object
   187	    @external_stub_object ||= @store.object_from_path(external_upload_stub.key)
   188	  end
   189	
   190	  def external_etag
   191	    @external_etag ||= external_stub_object.etag
   192	  end
   193	
   194	  def external_size
   195	    @external_size ||= external_stub_object.size
   196	  end
   197	
   198	  def external_sha1
   199	    @external_sha1 ||= external_stub_object.metadata["sha1-checksum"]
   200	  end
   201	
   202	  def download(key, type)
   203	    url = @store.signed_url_for_path(external_upload_stub.key, include_content_disposition: false)
   204	    uri = URI(url)
   205	    FileHelper.download(
   206	      url,
   207	      max_file_size: DOWNLOAD_LIMIT,
   208	      tmp_file_name: "discourse-upload-#{type}",
   209	      follow_redirect: true,
   210	      # Local S3 servers (like minio) do not use port 80, and the Aws::Sigv4::Signer
   211	      # includes the port number in the Host header when presigning URLs if the
   212	      # port is not 80, so we have to make sure the Host header sent by
   213	      # FinalDestination includes the port, otherwise we will get a
   214	      # `SignatureDoesNotMatch` error.
   215	      include_port_in_host_header: uri.scheme == "http" && uri.port != 80,
   216	    )
   217	  end
   218	end

     3	require "mini_mime"
     4	
     5	class UploadsController < ApplicationController
     6	  include ExternalUploadHelpers
     7	  include SecureUploadEndpointHelpers
     8	
     9	  requires_login except: %i[show show_short _show_secure_deprecated show_secure]
    10	
    11	  skip_before_action :preload_json,
    12	                     :check_xhr,
    13	                     :redirect_to_login_if_required,
    14	                     :redirect_to_profile_if_required,
    15	                     only: %i[show show_short _show_secure_deprecated show_secure]
    16	  protect_from_forgery except: :show

    18	  before_action :is_asset_path,

    21	  before_action :external_store_check, only: %i[_show_secure_deprecated show_secure]
    22	
    23	  SECURE_REDIRECT_GRACE_SECONDS = 5
    24	
    25	  def create
    26	    # capture current user for block later on
    27	    me = current_user
    28	    RateLimiter.new(
    29	      current_user,
    30	      "uploads-per-minute",
    31	      SiteSetting.max_uploads_per_minute,
    32	      1.minute,
    33	    ).performed!
    34	
    35	    type =
    36	      if params[:upload_type].presence
    37	        params[:upload_type]
    38	      elsif params[:type].presence
    39	        Discourse.deprecate(
    40	          "the :type param of `POST /uploads` is deprecated, use the :upload_type param instead",
    41	          since: "3.4",
    42	          drop_from: "3.5",
    43	        )
    44	        params[:type]
    45	      else
    46	        params.require(:upload_type)
    47	      end
    48	    # 50 characters ought to be enough for the upload type
    49	    type = type.parameterize(separator: "_")[0..50]
    50	
    51	    if type == "avatar" &&
    52	         (
    53	           SiteSetting.discourse_connect_overrides_avatar || SiteSetting.auth_overrides_avatar ||
    54	             !me.in_any_groups?(SiteSetting.uploaded_avatars_allowed_groups_map)
    55	         )
    56	      return render json: failed_json, status: :unprocessable_entity
    57	    end
    58	
    59	    url = params[:url]
    60	    file = params[:file] || params[:files]&.first
    61	    pasted = params[:pasted] == "true"
    62	    for_private_message = params[:for_private_message] == "true"
    63	    for_site_setting = params[:for_site_setting] == "true"
    64	    site_setting_name = for_site_setting ? params[:site_setting_name] : nil
    65	    is_api = is_api?
    66	    retain_hours = params[:retain_hours].to_i
    67	
    68	    # note, atm hijack is processed in its own context and has not access to controller
    69	    # longer term we may change this
    70	    hijack do
    71	      begin
    72	        info =
    73	          UploadsController.create_upload(
    74	            current_user: me,
    75	            file:,
    76	            url:,
    77	            type:,
    78	            for_private_message:,
    79	            for_site_setting:,
    80	            site_setting_name:,
    81	            pasted:,
    82	            is_api:,
    83	            retain_hours:,
    84	          )
    85	      rescue => e
    86	        render json: failed_json.merge(message: e.message&.split("\n")&.first),
    87	               status: :unprocessable_entity
    88	      else
    89	        render json: UploadsController.serialize_upload(info), status: Upload === info ? 200 : 422
    90	      end
    91	    end
    92	  end
    93	
    94	  def lookup_urls
    95	    params.permit(short_urls: [])
    96	    uploads = []
    97	
    98	    if (params[:short_urls] && params[:short_urls].length > 0)
    99	      PrettyText::Helpers
   100	        .lookup_upload_urls(params[:short_urls])
   101	        .each do |short_url, paths|
   102	          uploads << { short_url: short_url, url: paths[:url], short_path: paths[:short_path] }
   103	        end
   104	    end
   105	
   106	    render json: uploads.to_json
   107	  end
   108	
   109	  def show
   110	    # do not serve uploads requested via XHR to prevent XSS
   111	    return xhr_not_allowed if request.xhr?
   112	
   113	    return render_404 if !RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.has_db?(params[:site])
   114	
   115	    begin
   116	      request_site_current_user =
   117	        request.env.delete(Auth::DefaultCurrentUserProvider::CURRENT_USER_KEY)
   118	
   119	      RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.with_connection(params[:site]) do |db|
   120	        begin
   121	          # current_user here refers to the user for the site that we are operating on
   122	          # using with_connection. If DB for the target site matches the current site
   123	          # for the request, then current_user will be the same as the request_site_current_user
   124	          return render_404 if SiteSetting.prevent_anons_from_downloading_files && current_user.nil?
   125	
   126	          upload =
   127	            Upload.find_by(sha1: params[:sha]) ||
   128	              Upload.find_by(id: params[:id], url: request.env["PATH_INFO"])
   129	
   130	          if upload.present?
   131	            if !Discourse.store.internal?
   132	              local_store = FileStore::LocalStore.new
   133	              return render_404 unless local_store.has_been_uploaded?(upload.url)
   134	            end
   135	
   136	            send_file_local_upload(upload)
   137	          else
   138	            render_404
   139	          end
   140	        end
   141	      end
   142	    ensure
   143	      request.env[Auth::DefaultCurrentUserProvider::CURRENT_USER_KEY] = request_site_current_user
   144	    end
   145	  end
   146	
   147	  def show_short
   148	    # do not serve uploads requested via XHR to prevent XSS
   149	    return xhr_not_allowed if request.xhr?
   150	
   151	    return render_404 if SiteSetting.prevent_anons_from_downloading_files && current_user.nil?
   152	
   153	    sha1 = Upload.sha1_from_base62_encoded(params[:base62])
   154	
   155	    if upload = Upload.find_by(sha1: sha1)
   156	      return handle_secure_upload_request(upload) if upload.secure? && SiteSetting.secure_uploads?
   157	
   158	      if Discourse.store.internal?
   159	        send_file_local_upload(upload)
   160	      else
   161	        redirect_to Discourse.store.url_for(upload, force_download: force_download?),
   162	                    allow_other_host: true
   163	      end
   164	    else
   165	      render_404
   166	    end
   167	  end
   168	
   169	  # Kept to avoid rebaking old posts with /show-secure-uploads/ in their
   170	  # contents, this will ensure the uploads in these posts continue to
   171	  # work in future.
   172	  def _show_secure_deprecated
   173	    show_secure
   174	  end
   175	
   176	  def show_secure
   177	    # do not serve uploads requested via XHR to prevent XSS
   178	    return xhr_not_allowed if request.xhr?
   179	
   180	    path_with_ext =
   181	      params[:extension].nil? ? params[:path] : "#{params[:path]}.#{params[:extension]}"
   182	    upload = upload_from_path_and_extension(path_with_ext)
   183	
   184	    return render_404 if upload.blank?
   185	
   186	    return render_404 if SiteSetting.prevent_anons_from_downloading_files && current_user.nil?
   187	    return handle_secure_upload_request(upload, path_with_ext) if SiteSetting.secure_uploads?
   188	
   189	    # we don't want to 404 here if secure uploads gets disabled
   190	    # because all posts with secure uploads will show broken media
   191	    # until rebaked, which could take some time
   192	    #
   193	    # if the upload is still secure, that means the ACL is probably still
   194	    # private, so we don't want to go to the CDN url just yet otherwise we
   195	    # will get a 403. if the upload is not secure we assume the ACL is public
   196	    signed_secure_url =
   197	      Discourse.store.signed_url_for_path(
   198	        path_with_ext,
   199	        filename: upload.original_filename,
   200	        include_content_disposition: true,
   201	      )
   202	    redirect_to upload.secure? ? signed_secure_url : Discourse.store.cdn_url(upload.url),
   203	                allow_other_host: true
   204	  end
   205	
   206	  def handle_secure_upload_request(upload, path_with_ext = nil)
   207	    check_secure_upload_permission(upload)
   208	
   209	    # defaults to public: false, so only cached by the client browser
   210	    cache_seconds =
   211	      SiteSetting.s3_presigned_get_url_expires_after_seconds - SECURE_REDIRECT_GRACE_SECONDS
   212	    expires_in cache_seconds.seconds
   213	
   214	    # url_for figures out the full URL, handling multisite DBs,
   215	    # and will return a presigned URL for the upload
   216	    if path_with_ext.blank?
   217	      return(
   218	        redirect_to Discourse.store.url_for(upload, force_download: force_download?),
   219	                    allow_other_host: true
   220	      )
   221	    end
   222	
   223	    redirect_to Discourse.store.signed_url_for_path(
   224	                  path_with_ext,
   225	                  expires_in: SiteSetting.s3_presigned_get_url_expires_after_seconds,
   226	                  force_download: force_download?,
   227	                  filename: upload.original_filename,
   228	                  include_content_disposition: true,
   229	                ),
   230	                allow_other_host: true
   231	  end
   232	
   233	  def metadata
   234	    params.require(:url)
   235	    upload = Upload.get_from_url(params[:url])
   236	    raise Discourse::NotFound unless upload
   237	
   238	    render json: {
   239	             original_filename: upload.original_filename,
   240	             width: upload.width,
   241	             height: upload.height,
   242	             human_filesize: upload.human_filesize,
   243	           }
   244	  end

   256	  def validate_file_size(file_name:, file_size:)
   257	    raise ExternalUploadValidationError.new(I18n.t("upload.size_zero_failure")) if file_size.zero?
   258	
   259	    if attachment_too_big?(file_name, file_size)
   260	      raise ExternalUploadValidationError.new(
   261	              I18n.t(
   262	                "upload.attachments.too_large_humanized",
   263	                max_size:
   264	                  ActiveSupport::NumberHelper.number_to_human_size(
   265	                    UploadsController.max_attachment_size_for_user(current_user).kilobytes,
   266	                  ),
   267	              ),
   268	            )
   269	    end
   270	
   271	    if image_too_big?(file_name, file_size)

   273	              I18n.t(
   274	                "upload.images.too_large_humanized",
   275	                max_size:
   276	                  ActiveSupport::NumberHelper.number_to_human_size(
   277	                    SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb.kilobytes,
   278	                  ),
   279	              ),
   280	            )
   281	    end

   292	  def self.serialize_upload(data)
   293	    # as_json.as_json is not a typo... as_json in AM serializer returns keys as symbols, we need them
   294	    # as strings here
   295	    serialized = UploadSerializer.new(data, root: nil).as_json.as_json if Upload === data

   118	    def get_path_for(type, id, sha, extension)
   119	      depth = get_depth_for(id)
   120	      tree = File.join(*sha[0, depth].chars, "")
   121	      "#{type}/#{depth + 1}X/#{tree}#{sha}#{extension}"
   122	    end
   123	
   124	    def get_path_for_upload(upload)
   125	      # try to extract the path from the URL instead of calculating it,
   126	      # because the calculated path might differ from the actual path
   127	      if upload.url.present? && (path = upload.url[UPLOAD_PATH_REGEX, 1])
   128	        return prefix_path(path)
   129	      end
   130	
   131	      extension =
   132	        if upload.extension
   133	          ".#{upload.extension}"
   134	        else
   135	          # Maintain backward compatibility before Jobs::MigrateUploadExtensions runs
   136	          File.extname(upload.original_filename)
   137	        end
   138	
   139	      get_path_for("original", upload.id, upload.sha1, extension)
   140	    end
   141	
   142	    def get_path_for_optimized_image(optimized_image)
   143	      # try to extract the path from the URL instead of calculating it,
   144	      # because the calculated path might differ from the actual path
   145	      if optimized_image.url.present? && (path = optimized_image.url[OPTIMIZED_IMAGE_PATH_REGEX, 1])
   146	        return prefix_path(path)
   147	      end
   148	
   149	      upload = optimized_image.upload
   150	      version = optimized_image.version || 1
   151	      extension =
   152	        "_#{version}_#{optimized_image.width}x#{optimized_image.height}#{optimized_image.extension}"
   153	      get_path_for("optimized", upload.id, upload.sha1, extension)
   154	    end
   155	
   156	    CACHE_DIR = "#{Rails.root}/tmp/download_cache/"
   157	    CACHE_MAXIMUM_SIZE = 500
   158	    CACHE_EVICT_COUNT = 100
   159	
   160	    def get_cache_path_for(filename)
   161	      "#{CACHE_DIR}#{filename}"
   162	    end
   163	
   164	    def get_from_cache(filename)
   165	      path = get_cache_path_for(filename)
   166	      path if File.exist?(path)
   167	    end
   168	
   169	    def cache_file(file, filename)
   170	      path = get_cache_path_for(filename)
   171	      dir = File.dirname(path)
   172	      FileUtils.mkdir_p(dir) unless Dir.exist?(dir)
   173	      FileUtils.cp(file.path, path)
   174	
   175	      DiskCacheEviction.evict(
   176	        dir: CACHE_DIR,
   177	        max_entries: CACHE_MAXIMUM_SIZE,
   178	        evict_count: CACHE_EVICT_COUNT,
   179	      )
   180	    end
   181	
   182	    private
   183	
   184	    def perform_download(object, max_file_size_kb: nil)
   185	      DistributedMutex.synchronize("download_#{object.sha1}", validity: 3.minutes) do
   186	        name = object.respond_to?(:original_filename) ? object.original_filename : object.url
   187	        extension = File.extname(name)
   188	        filename = "#{object.sha1}#{extension}"
   189	
   190	        unless File.exist?(get_cache_path_for(filename))
   191	          max_file_size_kb ||= [
   192	            SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb,
   193	            SiteSetting.max_attachment_size_kb,
   194	          ].max.kilobytes
   195	
   196	          if object.respond_to?(:secure) ? object.secure? : object.upload.secure?
   197	            url =
   198	              Discourse.store.signed_url_for_path(object.url, include_content_disposition: false)
   199	          else
   200	            url = Discourse.store.cdn_url(object.url)
   201	          end
   202	
   203	          url = "#{SiteSetting.scheme}:#{url}" if url =~ %r{\A//}
   204	
   205	          file =
   206	            FileHelper.download(
   207	              url,
   208	              max_file_size: max_file_size_kb,
   209	              tmp_file_name: "discourse-download",
   210	              follow_redirect: true,
   211	            )
   212	
   213	          return if file.nil?
   214	
   215	          cache_file(file, filename)
   216	        end
   217	
   218	        get_from_cache(filename)
   219	      end
   220	    end

The synchronous path is now clear: `UploadsController#create` hijacks the request, `UploadCreator#create_for` validates/transforms/builds the row, then `Discourse.store` writes the file. The asynchronous fanout starts from the model’s `after_create` video callback and separate post-processing jobs.

   426	  MAX_CONVERT_FORMAT_SECONDS = 20
   427	  def execute_convert(from, to, opts = {})
   428	    command = ["magick", from, "-auto-orient", "-background", "white", "-interlace", "none"]
   429	    command << "-flatten" unless opts[:flatten] == false
   430	    command << "-debug" << "all" if opts[:debug]
   431	    command << "-quality" << opts[:quality].to_s if opts[:quality]
   432	    command << to
   433	
   434	    Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
   435	      *command,
   436	      failure_message: I18n.t("upload.png_to_jpg_conversion_failure_message"),
   437	      timeout: MAX_CONVERT_FORMAT_SECONDS,
   438	    )
   439	  end

   448	        else

   509	    doc = Nokogiri.XML(@file)
   510	    doc.xpath(svg_allowlist_xpath).remove
   511	    doc.xpath("//@*[starts-with(name(), 'on')]").remove
   512	    doc
   513	      .css("use")
   514	      .each do |use_el|

   516	          use_el.remove_attribute("href") unless use_el.attr("href").starts_with?("#")
   517	        end
   518	        use_el.remove_attribute("xlink:href")

   557	  def crop!
   558	    max_pixel_ratio = Discourse::PIXEL_RATIOS.max
   559	    filename_with_correct_ext = "image.#{@image_info.type}"
   560	
   561	    case @opts[:type]
   562	    when "avatar"
   563	      width = height = Discourse.avatar_sizes.max
   564	      OptimizedImage.resize(
   565	        @file.path,
   566	        @file.path,
   567	        width,
   568	        height,
   569	        filename: filename_with_correct_ext,
   570	      )
   571	    when "profile_background"
   572	      max_width = 850 * max_pixel_ratio
   573	      width, height =
   574	        ImageSizer.resize(
   575	          @image_info.size[0],
   576	          @image_info.size[1],
   577	          max_width: max_width,
   578	          max_height: max_width,
   579	        )
   580	      OptimizedImage.downsize(
   581	        @file.path,
   582	        @file.path,
   583	        "#{width}x#{height}\>",
   584	        filename: filename_with_correct_ext,
   585	      )
   586	    when "card_background"
   587	      max_width = 590 * max_pixel_ratio
   588	      width, height =
   589	        ImageSizer.resize(
   590	          @image_info.size[0],
   591	          @image_info.size[1],
   592	          max_width: max_width,
   593	          max_height: max_width,
   594	        )
   595	      OptimizedImage.downsize(
   596	        @file.path,
   597	        @file.path,
   598	        "#{width}x#{height}\>",
   599	        filename: filename_with_correct_ext,
   600	      )
   601	    when "custom_emoji"
   602	      OptimizedImage.downsize(
   603	        @file.path,
   604	        @file.path,
   605	        "100x100\>",
   606	        filename: filename_with_correct_ext,
   607	      )
   608	    end
   609	
   610	    extract_image_info!
   611	  end
   612	
   613	  def should_optimize?
   614	    # GIF is too slow (plus, we'll soon be converting them to MP4)
   615	    # Optimizing SVG is useless
   616	    return false if @file.path =~ /\.(gif|svg)\z/i
   617	    # Safeguard for large PNGs
   618	    return pixels < 2_000_000 if @file.path =~ /\.png/i
   619	    # Everything else is fine!
   620	    true

     2	module Jobs
     3	  class ConvertVideo < ::Jobs::Base
     4	    sidekiq_options queue: "low", concurrency: 5
     5	    MAX_RETRIES = 5
     6	    RETRY_DELAY = 30.seconds
     7	
     8	    def execute(args)
     9	      return if args[:upload_id].blank?
    10	
    11	      upload = Upload.find_by(id: args[:upload_id])
    12	      return if upload.blank?
    13	
    14	      return if OptimizedVideo.exists?(upload_id: upload.id)
    15	
    16	      if upload.url.blank?
    17	        retry_count = args[:retry_count].to_i
    18	        if retry_count < MAX_RETRIES
    19	          Jobs.enqueue_in(RETRY_DELAY, :convert_video, args.merge(retry_count: retry_count + 1))
    20	          return
    21	        else
    22	          Rails.logger.error(
    23	            "Upload #{upload.id} URL remained blank after #{MAX_RETRIES} retries when optimizing video",
    24	          )
    25	          return
    26	        end
    27	      end
    28	
    29	      adapter = VideoConversion::AdapterFactory.get_adapter(upload)
    30	
    31	      Rails.logger.error("Video conversion failed for upload #{upload.id}") if !adapter.convert
    32	    end
    33	  end
    34	end

     2	module Jobs
     3	  class CheckVideoConversionStatus < ::Jobs::Base
     4	    sidekiq_options queue: "low", concurrency: 5
     5	
     6	    def execute(args)
     7	      return if args[:upload_id].blank? || args[:job_id].blank? || args[:adapter_type].blank?
     8	
     9	      upload = Upload.find_by(id: args[:upload_id])
    10	      return if upload.blank?
    11	
    12	      adapter =
    13	        VideoConversion::AdapterFactory.get_adapter(upload, adapter_type: args[:adapter_type])
    14	
    15	      status = adapter.check_status(args[:job_id])
    16	
    17	      case status
    18	      when :complete
    19	        if adapter.handle_completion(args[:job_id], args[:new_sha1])
    20	          # Successfully completed
    21	          Rails.logger.info(
    22	            "Completed video conversion for upload ID #{upload.id} and job ID #{args[:job_id]}",
    23	          )
    24	        else
    25	          # Handle completion failed
    26	          Rails.logger.error(
    27	            "Failed to handle video conversion completion for upload ID #{upload.id} and job ID #{args[:job_id]}",
    28	          )
    29	        end
    30	      when :error
    31	        Rails.logger.error(
    32	          "Video conversion job failed for upload ID #{upload.id} and job ID #{args[:job_id]}",
    33	        )
    34	      when :pending
    35	        # Re-enqueue the job to check again
    36	        Jobs.enqueue_in(30.seconds, :check_video_conversion_status, args)
    37	      end
    38	    end
    39	  end
    40	end

    73	    # File is an actual Tempfile on disk
    74	    #
    75	    # An existing_external_upload_key is given for cases where move_existing is specified.
    76	    # This is an object already uploaded directly to S3 that we are now moving
    77	    # to its final resting place with the correct sha and key.
    78	    #
    79	    # options
    80	    #   - filename
    81	    #   - content_type
    82	    #   - cache_locally
    83	    #   - move_existing
    84	    #   - private

    86	    def store_file(file, path, opts = {})
    87	      path = path.dup
    88	
    89	      filename = opts[:filename].presence || File.basename(path)
    90	      # cache file locally when needed
    91	      cache_file(file, File.basename(path)) if opts[:cache_locally]
    92	
    93	      cache_control = "max-age=#{SiteSetting.s3_max_age}, public, immutable"
    94	      if SiteSetting.s3_stale_while_revalidate != SiteSetting.defaults[:s3_stale_while_revalidate]
    95	        cache_control =
    96	          "#{cache_control}, stale-while-revalidate=#{SiteSetting.s3_stale_while_revalidate}"
    97	      end
    98	
    99	      options = {
   100	        cache_control: cache_control,
   101	        content_type:
   102	          opts[:content_type].presence || MiniMime.lookup_by_filename(filename)&.content_type,
   103	      }.merge(default_s3_options(secure: opts[:private]))
   104	
   105	      # Only serve inline for allowlisted safe file types (non-SVG images and PDFs)
   106	      # to prevent XSS via HTML/XML/SVG uploads. All other files force download.
   107	      # See https://github.com/discourse/discourse/commit/31e31ef44973dc4daaee2f010d71588ea5873b53
   108	      options[:content_disposition] = self.class.content_disposition_for(filename)
   109	
   110	      path.prepend(File.join(upload_path, "/")) if Rails.configuration.multisite
   111	
   112	      # if this fails, it will throw an exception
   113	      if opts[:move_existing] && opts[:existing_external_upload_key]
   114	        original_path = opts[:existing_external_upload_key]
   115	        options[:apply_metadata_to_destination] = true
   116	        path, etag = s3_helper.copy(original_path, path, options: options)
   117	        delete_file(original_path)
   118	      else
   119	        path, etag = s3_helper.upload(file, path, options)
   120	      end
   121	
   122	      # return the upload url and etag
   123	      [File.join(absolute_base_url, path), etag]
   124	    end

   142	    def has_been_uploaded?(url)
   143	      return false if url.blank?
   144	
   145	      begin
   146	        parsed_url = URI.parse(UrlHelper.encode(url))
   147	      rescue StandardError
   148	        # There are many exceptions possible here including Addressable::URI:: exceptions
   149	        # and URI:: exceptions, catch all may seem wide, but it makes no sense to raise ever
   150	        # on an invalid url here
   151	        return false
   152	      end
   153	
   154	      base_hostname = URI.parse(absolute_base_url).hostname
   155	      if url[base_hostname]
   156	        # if the hostnames match it means the upload is in the same
   157	        # bucket on s3. however, the bucket folder path may differ in
   158	        # some cases, and we do not want to assume the url is uploaded
   159	        # here. e.g. the path of the current site could be /prod and the
   160	        # other site could be /staging
   161	        if s3_bucket_folder_path.present?
   162	          return parsed_url.path.starts_with?("/#{s3_bucket_folder_path}")
   163	        else
   164	          return true
   165	        end
   166	      end
   167	
   168	      return false if SiteSetting.Upload.s3_cdn_url.blank?
   169	
   170	      s3_cdn_url = URI.parse(SiteSetting.Upload.s3_cdn_url || "")
   171	      cdn_hostname = s3_cdn_url.hostname
   172	
   173	      if cdn_hostname.presence && url[cdn_hostname] &&
   174	           (s3_cdn_url.path.blank? || parsed_url.path.starts_with?(s3_cdn_url.path))
   175	        return true
   176	      end
   177	      false
   178	    end

   353	    def copy_from(source_path)
   354	      local_store = FileStore::LocalStore.new
   355	      public_upload_path = File.join(local_store.public_dir, local_store.upload_path)
   356	
   357	      # The migration to S3 and lots of other code expects files to exist in public/uploads,
   358	      # so lets move them there before executing the migration.
   359	      if public_upload_path != source_path
   360	        if Dir.exist?(public_upload_path)
   361	          old_upload_path = "#{public_upload_path}_#{SecureRandom.hex}"
   362	          FileUtils.mv(public_upload_path, old_upload_path)
   363	        end
   364	      end
   365	
   366	      FileUtils.mkdir_p(File.expand_path("..", public_upload_path))
   367	      FileUtils.symlink(source_path, public_upload_path)
   368	
   369	      FileStore::ToS3Migration.new(
   370	        s3_options: FileStore::ToS3Migration.s3_options_from_site_settings,
   371	        migrate_to_multisite: Rails.configuration.multisite,
   372	      ).migrate
   373	    ensure
   374	      FileUtils.rm(public_upload_path) if File.symlink?(public_upload_path)
   375	      FileUtils.mv(old_upload_path, public_upload_path) if old_upload_path
   376	    end

   383	    # The following are canned ACLs defined by AWS S3 and not some generic value which we decide to use.

   388	    def self.acl_option_value(secure:)

   397	    def self.visibility_tagging_option_value(secure:, encode_form: true)
   398	      return if !SiteSetting.s3_enable_access_control_tags
   399	
   400	      key = SiteSetting.s3_access_control_tag_key
   401	      return if key.blank?
   402	
   403	      option_value = {
   404	        key =>
   405	          (
   406	            if secure
   407	              SiteSetting.s3_access_control_tag_private_value
   408	            else
   409	              SiteSetting.s3_access_control_tag_public_value
   410	            end
   411	          ),
   412	      }
   413	
   414	      encode_form ? URI.encode_www_form(option_value) : option_value

   417	    def self.default_s3_options(secure:)
   418	      options = {}
   419

   422	      end
   423	
   424	      if tagging_option_value = visibility_tagging_option_value(secure:)

   434	
   435	    # S3 limits total request header/metadata size to 2 KB. Content-Disposition
   436	    # includes the filename twice (filename= and filename*=UTF-8''), so we
   437	    # limit the full header to 1600 bytes to stay within that budget.
   438	    MAX_CONTENT_DISPOSITION_BYTES = 1_600
   439	
   440	    def self.content_disposition_for(filename, disposition: nil)
   441	      disposition ||= FileHelper.is_inline_safe?(filename) ? "inline" : "attachment"
   442	      return "" if filename.blank?
   443	
   444	      header = ActionDispatch::Http::ContentDisposition.format(disposition:, filename:)
   445	      return header if header.bytesize <= MAX_CONTENT_DISPOSITION_BYTES
   446	
   447	      extension = File.extname(filename)
   448	      basename = File.basename(filename, extension)
   449	
   450	      # Binary search for the longest basename that fits within the limit
   451	      low = 1
   452	      high = basename.length
   453	
   454	      while low < high
   455	        mid = (low + high + 1) / 2
   456	        candidate = "#{basename[0...mid]}#{extension}"
   457	        test_header =
   458	          ActionDispatch::Http::ContentDisposition.format(disposition:, filename: candidate)
   459	        if test_header.bytesize <= MAX_CONTENT_DISPOSITION_BYTES
   460	          low = mid
   461	        else
   462	          high = mid - 1
   463	        end
   464	      end
   465	
   466	      ActionDispatch::Http::ContentDisposition.format(
   467	        disposition:,
   468	        filename: "#{basename[0...low]}#{extension}",
   469	      )
   470	    end

   474	    def presigned_get_url(
   475	      url,
   476	      force_download: false,
   477	      filename: false,
   478	      expires_in: SiteSetting.s3_presigned_get_url_expires_after_seconds
   479	    )
   480	      opts = { expires_in: expires_in }
   481	
   482	      if filename
   483	        disposition =
   484	          (force_download || !FileHelper.is_inline_safe?(filename)) ? "attachment" : "inline"
   485	        opts[:response_content_disposition] = ActionDispatch::Http::ContentDisposition.format(
   486	          disposition:,
   487	          filename:,
   488	        )
   489	      end
   490	
   491	      obj = object_from_path(url)
   492	      obj.presigned_url(:get, opts)
   493	    end

   500	      end
   501	    end

   503	    def update_access_control(key, secure, remove_existing_acl: false)
   504	      acl = self.class.acl_option_value(secure:)
   505	
   506	      if acl.present? || remove_existing_acl
   507	        begin
   508	          object_from_path(key).acl.put(acl:)
   509	        rescue Aws::S3::Errors::NotImplemented => err
   510	          Discourse.warn_exception(
   511	            err,
   512	            message: "The file store object storage provider does not support setting ACLs",
   513	          )
   514	        end
   515	      end
   516	
   517	      if tagging_option_value =
   518	           self.class.visibility_tagging_option_value(secure:, encode_form: false)
   519	        s3_helper.upsert_tag(
   520	          key,
   521	          tag_key: tagging_option_value.keys.first,
   522	          tag_value: tagging_option_value.values.first,
   523	        )
   524	      end
   525	    rescue Aws::S3::Errors::NoSuchKey
   526	      Rails.logger.warn(
   527	        "Could not update access control on upload with key: '#{key}'. Upload is missing.",
   528	      )
   529	    end

     3	class OptimizedImage < ActiveRecord::Base
     4	  include HasUrl
     5	  belongs_to :upload
     6	
     7	  # BUMP UP if optimized image algorithm changes
     8	  VERSION = 2
     9	  URL_REGEX = %r{(/optimized/\dX[/\.\w]*/([a-zA-Z0-9]+)[\.\w]*)}
    10	
    11	  def self.lock(upload_id, width, height)
    12	    @hostname ||= Discourse.os_hostname
    13	    # note, the extra lock here ensures we only optimize one image per machine on webs
    14	    # this can very easily lead to runaway CPU so slowing it down is beneficial and it is hijacked
    15	    #
    16	    # we can not afford this blocking in Sidekiq cause it can lead to starvation
    17	    if lock_per_machine?
    18	      DistributedMutex.synchronize("optimized_image_host_#{@hostname}") do
    19	        DistributedMutex.synchronize("optimized_image_#{upload_id}_#{width}_#{height}") { yield }
    20	      end
    21	    else
    22	      DistributedMutex.synchronize("optimized_image_#{upload_id}_#{width}_#{height}") { yield }
    23	    end
    24	  end
    25	
    26	  def self.lock_per_machine?
    27	    return @lock_per_machine if defined?(@lock_per_machine)
    28	    @lock_per_machine = !Sidekiq.server?
    29	  end
    30	
    31	  def self.lock_per_machine=(value)
    32	    @lock_per_machine = value
    33	  end
    34	
    35	  def self.create_for(upload, width, height, opts = {})
    36	    return if width <= 0 || height <= 0
    37	    return if upload.try(:sha1).blank?
    38	
    39	    # no extension so try to guess it
    40	    upload.fix_image_extension if (!upload.extension)
    41	
    42	    if !upload.extension.match?(IM_DECODERS)
    43	      if opts[:raise_on_error]
    44	        raise InvalidAccess
    45	      else
    46	        # nothing to do ... bad extension, not an image
    47	        return
    48	      end
    49	    end
    50	
    51	    # prefer to look up the thumbnail without grabbing any locks
    52	    extension = ".#{opts[:format] || upload.extension}"
    53	    thumbnail = find_by(upload_id: upload.id, width: width, height: height, extension: extension)
    54	
    55	    # correct bad thumbnail if needed
    56	    if thumbnail && (thumbnail.url.blank? || thumbnail.version != VERSION)
    57	      thumbnail.destroy!
    58	      thumbnail = nil
    59	    end
    60	
    61	    return thumbnail if thumbnail
    62	
    63	    store = Discourse.store
    64	
    65	    # create the thumbnail otherwise
    66	    original_path = store.path_for(upload)
    67	
    68	    if original_path.blank?
    69	      # download is protected with a DistributedMutex
    70	      original_path = store.download(upload)
    71	    end
    72	
    73	    if extension == ".svg" && upload.extension != "svg"
    74	      if opts[:raise_on_error]
    75	        raise InvalidAccess
    76	      else
    77	        # we can not convert any images to svg, unsupported
    78	        return
    79	      end
    80	    end
    81	
    82	    lock(upload.id, width, height) do
    83	      # may have been generated since we got the lock
    84	      thumbnail = find_by(upload_id: upload.id, width: width, height: height, extension: extension)
    85	
    86	      # return the previous thumbnail if any
    87	      return thumbnail if thumbnail
    88	
    89	      if original_path.blank?
    90	        Rails.logger.error("Could not find file in the store located at url: #{upload.url}")
    91	      else
    92	        # create a temp file with the same extension as the original
    93	
    94	        return nil if extension.length == 1
    95	
    96	        temp_file = Tempfile.new(["discourse-thumbnail", extension])
    97	        temp_path = temp_file.path
    98	
    99	        target_quality =
   100	          upload.target_image_quality(
   101	            original_path,
   102	            SiteSetting.ImageQuality.image_preview_jpg_quality,
   103	          )
   104	        opts = opts.merge(quality: target_quality) if target_quality
   105	        opts = opts.merge(upload_id: upload.id)
   106	
   107	        # special case, when "resizing" vectors we simply copy
   108	        if extension == ".svg"
   109	          FileUtils.cp(original_path, temp_path)
   110	          resized = true
   111	        elsif opts[:crop]
   112	          resized = crop(original_path, temp_path, width, height, opts)
   113	        else
   114	          resized = resize(original_path, temp_path, width, height, opts)
   115	        end
   116	
   117	        if resized
   118	          # TODO: crop vs resize should be stored in the db, quality should be stored
   119	          thumbnail =
   120	            OptimizedImage.create!(
   121	              upload_id: upload.id,
   122	              sha1: Upload.generate_digest(temp_path),
   123	              extension: extension,
   124	              width: width,
   125	              height: height,
   126	              url: "",
   127	              filesize: File.size(temp_path),
   128	              version: VERSION,
   129	            )
   130	
   131	          # store the optimized image and update its url
   132	          File.open(temp_path) do |file|
   133	            url = store.store_optimized_image(file, thumbnail, nil, secure: upload.secure?)
   134	            if url.present?
   135	              thumbnail.url = url
   136	              thumbnail.save
   137	            else
   138	              Rails.logger.error(
   139	                "Failed to store optimized image of size #{width}x#{height} from url: #{upload.url}\nTemp image path: #{temp_path}",
   140	              )
   141	            end
   142	          end
   143	        end
   144	
   145	        # close && remove temp file
   146	        temp_file.close!
   147	      end
   148	
   149	      thumbnail
   150	    end
   151	  end
   152	
   153	  def destroy
   154	    OptimizedImage.transaction do
   155	      Discourse.store.remove_optimized_image(self) if self.upload
   156	      super
   157	    end
   158	  end
   159	
   160	  def local?
   161	    !(url =~ %r{\A(https?:)?//})
   162	  end
   163	
   164	  def calculate_filesize
   165	    path =
   166	      if local?
   167	        Discourse.store.path_for(self)
   168	      else
   169	        Discourse.store.download!(self)
   170	      end
   171	    File.size(path)
   172	  end
   173	
   174	  def filesize
   175	    if size = read_attribute(:filesize)
   176	      size
   177	    else
   178	      size = calculate_filesize
   179	
   180	      self[:filesize] = size
   181	      update_columns(filesize: size) if !new_record?
   182	      size
   183	    end
   184	  end
   185	
   186	  def self.safe_path?(path)
   187	    # this matches instructions which call #to_s
   188	    path = path.to_s
   189	    return false if path != File.expand_path(path)
   190	    return false if path !~ %r{\A[\w\-\./]+\z}m
   191	    true
   192	  end
   193	
   194	  def self.ensure_safe_paths!(*paths)
   195	    paths.each { |path| raise Discourse::InvalidAccess unless safe_path?(path) }
   196	  end
   197	
   198	  IM_DECODERS = /\A(jpe?g|png|ico|gif|webp|avif|svg)\z/i
   199	
   200	  def self.prepend_decoder!(path, ext_path = nil, opts = nil)
   201	    opts ||= {}
   202	
   203	    # This logic is a little messy but the result of using mocks for most
   204	    # of the image tests. The idea here is you shouldn't trust the "original"
   205	    # path of a file to figure out its extension. However, in certain cases
   206	    # such as generating the loading upload thumbnail, we force the format,
   207	    # and this allows us to use the forced format in that case.
   208	    extension = nil
   209	    if (opts[:format] && path != ext_path)
   210	      extension = File.extname(path)[1..-1]
   211	    else
   212	      extension = File.extname(opts[:filename] || ext_path || path)[1..-1]
   213	    end
   214	
   215	    if !extension || !extension.match?(IM_DECODERS)
   216	      raise Discourse::InvalidAccess.new("Unsupported extension: #{extension}")
   217	    end
   218	    "#{extension}:#{path}"
   219	  end

     3	class UploadReference < ActiveRecord::Base
     4	  belongs_to :upload
     5	  belongs_to :target, polymorphic: true
     6	
     7	  delegate :to_markdown, to: :upload
     8	
     9	  def self.ensure_exist!(upload_ids: [], target: nil, target_type: nil, target_id: nil)
    10	    if !target && !(target_type && target_id)
    11	      raise "target OR target_type and target_id are required"
    12	    end
    13	
    14	    if target.present?
    15	      target_type = target.class
    16	      target_id = target.id
    17	    end
    18	
    19	    upload_ids = upload_ids.uniq.reject(&:blank?)
    20	    target_type = target_type.to_s
    21	
    22	    if upload_ids.empty?
    23	      UploadReference.where(target_type: target_type, target_id: target_id).delete_all
    24	
    25	      return
    26	    end
    27	
    28	    rows =
    29	      upload_ids.map do |upload_id|
    30	        {
    31	          upload_id: upload_id,
    32	          target_type: target_type,
    33	          target_id: target_id,
    34	          created_at: Time.zone.now,
    35	          updated_at: Time.zone.now,
    36	        }
    37	      end
    38	
    39	    UploadReference.transaction do |transaction|
    40	      UploadReference
    41	        .where(target_type: target_type, target_id: target_id)
    42	        .where.not(upload_id: upload_ids)
    43	        .delete_all
    44	
    45	      UploadReference.insert_all(rows)
    46	    end
    47	  end
    48	end

     6	  def link_post_uploads(fragments: nil)
     7	    upload_ids = []
     8	
     9	    each_upload_url(fragments: fragments) do |url, _, sha1|
    10	      upload = Upload.fetch_from(sha1:, url:)
    11	
    12	      # Link any video thumbnails
    13	      if SiteSetting.video_thumbnails_enabled && upload.present? &&
    14	           FileHelper.supported_video.include?(upload.extension&.downcase)
    15	        # Video thumbnails have the filename of the video file sha1 with a .png or .jpg extension.
    16	        # This is because at time of upload in the composer we don't know the topic/post id yet
    17	        # and there is no thumbnail info added to the markdown to tie the thumbnail to the topic/post after
    18	        # creation.
    19	        thumbnail =
    20	          Upload
    21	            .where("original_filename like ?", "#{upload.sha1}.%")
    22	            .order(id: :desc)
    23	            .first if upload.sha1.present?
    24	        if thumbnail.present?
    25	          upload_ids << thumbnail.id
    26	          handle_video_thumbnail(thumbnail) if respond_to?(:handle_video_thumbnail, true)
    27	        end
    28	      end
    29	      upload_ids << upload.id if upload.present?
    30	    end
    31	
    32	    upload_references =
    33	      upload_ids.map do |upload_id|
    34	        {
    35	          target_id: self.id,
    36	          target_type: self.class.name,
    37	          upload_id: upload_id,
    38	          created_at: Time.zone.now,
    39	          updated_at: Time.zone.now,
    40	        }
    41	      end
    42	
    43	    UploadReference.transaction do
    44	      UploadReference.where(target: self).delete_all
    45	      UploadReference.insert_all(upload_references) if upload_references.size > 0
    46	
    47	      if SiteSetting.secure_uploads?
    48	        Upload
    49	          .where(id: upload_ids, access_control_post_id: nil)
    50	          .where("id NOT IN (SELECT upload_id FROM custom_emojis)")
    51	          .update_all(access_control_post_id: access_control_post_id_for_upload)
    52	      end
    53	    end
    54	  end

     3	# Post processing that we can do after a post has already been cooked.
     4	# For example, inserting the onebox content, or image sizes/thumbnails.
     5	
     6	class CookedPostProcessor
     7	  include CookedProcessorMixin
     8	
     9	  attr_reader :cooking_options, :doc
    10	
    11	  def initialize(post, opts = {})
    12	    @dirty = false
    13	    @opts = opts
    14	    @post = post
    15	    @model = post
    16	    @previous_cooked = (@post.cooked || "").dup
    17	    # NOTE: we re-cook the post here in order to prevent timing issues with edits
    18	    # cf. https://meta.discourse.org/t/edit-of-rebaked-post-doesnt-show-in-html-only-in-raw/33815/6
    19	    @cooking_options = post.cooking_options || opts[:cooking_options] || {}
    20	    @cooking_options[:topic_id] = post.topic_id
    21	    @cooking_options = @cooking_options.symbolize_keys
    22	    @should_secure_uploads = @post.should_secure_uploads?
    23	    @category_id = @post&.topic&.category_id
    24	
    25	    cooked = post.cook(post.raw, @cooking_options)
    26	    @doc = Loofah.html5_fragment(cooked)
    27	    @has_oneboxes = post.post_analyzer.found_oneboxes?
    28	    @size_cache = {}
    29	
    30	    @disable_dominant_color = !!opts[:disable_dominant_color]
    31	    @omit_nofollow = post.omit_nofollow?
    32	  end
    33	
    34	  def post_process(new_post: false)
    35	    DistributedMutex.synchronize("post_process_#{@post.id}", validity: 10.minutes) do
    36	      DiscourseEvent.trigger(:before_post_process_cooked, @doc, @post)
    37	      update_uploads_secure_status
    38	      remove_full_quote_on_direct_reply if new_post
    39	      post_process_oneboxes
    40	      post_process_images
    41	      post_process_videos
    42	      add_blocked_hotlinked_media_placeholders
    43	      post_process_quotes
    44	      optimize_urls
    45	      remove_user_ids
    46	      update_post_image
    47	      enforce_nofollow
    48	      grant_badges
    49	      @post.link_post_uploads(fragments: @doc)
    50	      DiscourseEvent.trigger(:post_process_cooked, @doc, @post)
    51	      nil
    52	    end
    53	  end
    54	
    55	  def has_emoji?
    56	    (@doc.css("img.emoji") - @doc.css(".quote img")).size > 0
    57	  end
    58	
    59	  def grant_badges
    60	    return if @post.user.blank? || !Guardian.new.can_see?(@post)
    61	
    62	    BadgeGranter.grant(Badge.find(Badge::FirstEmoji), @post.user, post_id: @post.id) if has_emoji?
    63	    if @has_oneboxes
    64	      BadgeGranter.grant(Badge.find(Badge::FirstOnebox), @post.user, post_id: @post.id)
    65	    end
    66	    if @post.is_reply_by_email?
    67	      BadgeGranter.grant(Badge.find(Badge::FirstReplyByEmail), @post.user, post_id: @post.id)
    68	    end
    69	  end
    70	
    71	  def post_process_quotes
    72	    @doc
    73	      .css("aside.quote")
    74	      .each do |q|
    75	        post_number = q["data-post"]
    76	        topic_id = q["data-topic"]
    77	        if topic_id && post_number
    78	          comparer = QuoteComparer.new(topic_id.to_i, post_number.to_i, q.css("blockquote").text)
    79	
    80	          q["class"] = ((q["class"] || "") + " quote-post-not-found").strip if comparer.missing?
    81	          q["class"] = ((q["class"] || "") + " quote-modified").strip if comparer.modified?
    82	        end
    83	      end
    84	  end
    85	
    86	  def update_uploads_secure_status
    87	    @post.update_uploads_secure_status(source: "post processor")
    88	  end
    89	
    90	  def remove_full_quote_on_direct_reply
    91	    return if !SiteSetting.remove_full_quote
    92	    return if @post.post_number == 1
    93	    return if @doc.xpath("aside[contains(@class, 'quote')]").size != 1
    94	
    95	    previous =
    96	      Post
    97	        .where(
    98	          "post_number < ? AND topic_id = ? AND post_type = ? AND NOT hidden",
    99	          @post.post_number,
   100	          @post.topic_id,
   101	          Post.types[:regular],
   102	        )
   103	        .order("post_number DESC")
   104	        .limit(1)
   105	        .pluck(:cooked)
   106	        .first
   107	
   108	    return if previous.blank?
   109	
   110	    previous_text = Nokogiri::HTML5.fragment(previous).text.strip
   111	    quoted_text = @doc.css("aside.quote:first-child blockquote").first&.text&.strip || ""
   112	
   113	    return if previous_text.gsub(/(\s){2,}/, '\1') != quoted_text.gsub(/(\s){2,}/, '\1')
   114	
   115	    quote_regexp = %r{\A\s*\[quote.+\[/quote\]}im
   116	    quoteless_raw = @post.raw.sub(quote_regexp, "").strip
   117	
   118	    return if @post.raw.strip == quoteless_raw
   119	
   120	    PostRevisor.new(@post).revise!(
   121	      Discourse.system_user,
   122	      { raw: quoteless_raw, edit_reason: I18n.t(:removed_direct_reply_full_quotes) },
   123	      skip_validations: true,
   124	      bypass_bump: true,
   125	    )
   126	  end

   129	    # all images with a src attribute
   130	    @doc.css("img[src]") -
   131	      # minus emojis
   132	      @doc.css("img.emoji") -
   133	      # minus images inside quotes
   134	      @doc.css(".quote img") -
   135	      # minus onebox site icons
   136	      @doc.css("img.site-icon") -
   137	      # minus onebox avatars
   138	      @doc.css("img.onebox-avatar") - @doc.css("img.onebox-avatar-inline") -
   139	      # minus github onebox profile images
   140	      @doc.css(".onebox.githubfolder img")
   141	  end

   143	  def update_post_image
   144	    upload = nil
   145	    images = extract_images_for_post
   146	
   147	    @post.each_upload_url(fragments: images.css("[data-thumbnail]")) do |url, path, sha1|
   148	      upload = Upload.fetch_from(sha1:, url:)
   149	      break if upload
   150	    end
   151	
   152	    if upload.nil? # No specified thumbnail. Use any image:
   153	      @post.each_upload_url(fragments: images.css(":not([data-thumbnail])")) do |url, path, sha1|
   154	        upload = Upload.fetch_from(sha1:, url:)
   155	        break if upload
   156	      end
   157	    end
   158	
   159	    if upload.present?
   160	      @post.update_column(:image_upload_id, upload.id) # post
   161	      if @post.is_first_post? # topic
   162	        @post.topic.update_column(:image_upload_id, upload.id)
   163	        extra_sizes =
   164	          ThemeModifierHelper.new(theme_ids: Theme.user_selectable.pluck(:id)).topic_thumbnail_sizes
   165	        @post.topic.generate_thumbnails!(extra_sizes: extra_sizes)
   166	      end
   167	    else
   168	      @post.update_column(:image_upload_id, nil) if @post.image_upload_id
   169	      if @post.topic.image_upload_id && @post.is_first_post?
   170	        @post.topic.update_column(:image_upload_id, nil)
   171	      end
   172	      nil
   173	    end
   174	  end
   175	
   176	  def optimize_urls
   177	    %w[href data-download-href].each do |selector|
   178	      @doc.css("a[#{selector}]").each { |a| a[selector] = UrlHelper.cook_url(a[selector].to_s) }
   179	    end
   180	
   181	    %w[src].each do |selector|
   182	      @doc
   183	        .css("img[#{selector}], video[#{selector}]")
   184	        .each do |img|
   185	          custom_emoji = img["class"]&.include?("emoji-custom") && Emoji.custom?(img["title"])
   186	          img[selector] = UrlHelper.cook_url(
   187	            img[selector].to_s,
   188	            secure: @should_secure_uploads && !custom_emoji,
   189	          )
   190	        end
   191	    end
   192	  end
   193	
   194	  def remove_user_ids
   195	    @doc
   196	      .css("a[href]")
   197	      .each do |a|
   198	        uri =
   199	          begin
   200	            URI(a["href"])
   201	          rescue URI::Error
   202	            next
   203	          end
   204	        next if uri.hostname != Discourse.current_hostname
   205	
   206	        query = Rack::Utils.parse_nested_query(uri.query)
   207	        next if !query.delete("u")
   208	
   209	        uri.query = query.map { |k, v| "#{k}=#{v}" }.join("&").presence
   210	        a["href"] = uri.to_s
   211	      end
   212	  end
   213	
   214	  def enforce_nofollow
   215	    add_nofollow = !@omit_nofollow && SiteSetting.add_rel_nofollow_to_user_content
   216	    PrettyText.add_rel_attributes_to_user_content(@doc, add_nofollow)
   217	  end
