   219	  private
   220	
   221	  def post_process_videos
   222	    changes_made = false
   223	
   224	    begin
   225	      @doc
   226	        .css(".video-placeholder-container")
   227	        .each do |container|
   228	          src = container["data-video-src"]
   229	          next if src.blank?
   230	
   231	          # Look for optimized video
   232	          upload = Upload.get_from_url(src)
   233	          if upload && optimized_video = OptimizedVideo.find_by(upload_id: upload.id)
   234	            optimized_url = Discourse.store.cdn_url(optimized_video.optimized_upload.url)
   235	            # Only update if the URL is different
   236	            if container["data-video-src"] != optimized_url
   237	              container["data-original-video-src"] = container["data-video-src"] unless container[
   238	                "data-original-video-src"
   239	              ]
   240	              container["data-video-src"] = optimized_url
   241	              changes_made = true
   242	            end
   243	            # Ensure we maintain reference to original upload
   244	            @post.link_post_uploads(fragments: @doc)
   245	          end
   246	        end
   247	
   248	      # Update the post's cooked content if changes were made
   249	      if changes_made
   250	        new_cooked = @doc.to_html
   251	        @post.cooked = new_cooked
   252	        if !@post.save
   253	          Rails.logger.error("Failed to save post: #{@post.errors.full_messages.join(", ")}")
   254	        end
   255	      end
   256	    rescue => e
   257	      Rails.logger.error("Error in post_process_videos: #{e.message}")
   258	      Rails.logger.error(e.backtrace.join("\n"))
   259	      raise
   260	    end
   261	  end
   262	
   263	  def add_blocked_hotlinked_media_placeholders
   264	    @doc
   265	      .css(
   266	        [
   267	          "[#{PrettyText::BLOCKED_HOTLINKED_SRC_ATTR}]",
   268	          "[#{PrettyText::BLOCKED_HOTLINKED_SRCSET_ATTR}]",
   269	        ].join(","),
   270	      )
   271	      .each do |el|
   272	        src =
   273	          el[PrettyText::BLOCKED_HOTLINKED_SRC_ATTR] ||
   274	            el[PrettyText::BLOCKED_HOTLINKED_SRCSET_ATTR]&.split(",")&.first&.split(" ")&.first
   275	
   276	        if el.name == "img"
   277	          add_blocked_hotlinked_image_placeholder!(el)
   278	          next
   279	        end

further after this 528 thumbnail = upload.thumbnail(w, h) 529 if thumbnail && thumbnail.filesize.to_i < upload.filesize 530 img["src"] = thumbnail.url 531 532 srcset = +"" 533 534 # Skip srcset for onebox images. 554 end 555 end 556 else 557 img["src"] = upload.url 558 end 559 560 if !@disable_dominant_color && 561 (color = upload.dominant_color(calculate_if_missing: true).presence) 562 img["data-dominant-color"] = color 563 end 564 end 565 566 def add_lightbox!(img, original_width, original_height, upload, crop) 567 return if original_width < MIN_LIGHTBOX_WIDTH || original_height < MIN_LIGHTBOX_HEIGHT 568 569 # first, create a div to hold our lightbox 570 lightbox = create_node("div", LIGHTBOX_WRAPPER_CSS_CLASS) 571 img.add_next_sibling(lightbox) 572 lightbox.add_child(img) 573 574 # then, the link to our larger image 575 src_url = Upload.secure_uploads_url?(img["src"]) ? upload&.url || img["src"] : img["src"] 576 src = UrlHelper.cook_url(src_url, secure: @should_secure_uploads) 577 578 a = create_link_node("lightbox", src) 579 img.add_next_sibling(a) 580 581 a["data-download-href"] = Discourse.store.download_url(upload) if upload 582 583 a.add_child(img) 584 585 # then, some overlay informations 586 meta = create_node("div", "meta") 587 img.add_next_sibling(meta) 588 589 filename = get_filename(upload, img["src"]) 590 informations = +"#{original_width}×#{original_height}" 591 informations << " #{upload.human_filesize}" if upload 592 593 a["title"] = img["title"] || img["alt"] || filename 594 595 meta.add_child create_icon_node("far-image") 596 meta.add_child create_span_node("filename", a["title"]) 597 meta.add_child create_span_node("informations", informations) 598 meta.add_child create_icon_node("discourse-expand") 599 end 600 end

The post/rendering surface is the riskiest cluster so far: it parses raw/cooked URLs, resolves them back to uploads, generates optimized images, links `UploadReference`, and rewrites cooked HTML. I’m checking storage, cleanup, email, backup, and theme/site-setting surfaces next.

     2	
     3	module Jobs
     4	  class CleanUpUploads < ::Jobs::Scheduled
     5	    every 1.hour
     6	
     7	    def execute(args)
     8	      grace_period = [SiteSetting.clean_orphan_uploads_grace_period_hours, 1].max
     9	
    10	      # Always remove invalid upload records regardless of clean_up_uploads setting.
    11	      Upload
    12	        .by_users
    13	        .where(
    14	          "retain_hours IS NULL OR created_at < current_timestamp - interval '1 hour' * retain_hours",
    15	        )
    16	        .where("created_at < ?", grace_period.hour.ago)
    17	        .where(url: "")
    18	        .find_each(&:destroy!)
    19	
    20	      return unless SiteSetting.clean_up_uploads?
    21	
    22	      # Do nothing if the last cleanup was run too recently.
    23	      last_cleanup_timestamp = last_cleanup
    24	      if last_cleanup_timestamp.present? &&
    25	           (Time.zone.now.to_i - last_cleanup_timestamp) < (grace_period / 2).hours
    26	        return
    27	      end
    28	
    29	      result = Upload.by_users
    30	      Upload.unused_callbacks&.each { |handler| result = handler.call(result) }
    31	
    32	      # 1. Exclude uploads that have retain_hours set and are still within that retention period.
    33	      # 2. Exclude uploads created in the grace period.
    34	      # 3. Exclude secure uploads that have an access_control_post_id.
    35	      # 4. Exclude uploads that are link to an upload reference.
    36	      # 5. Exclude uploads that are linked to anything but a Post via UploadReference.
    37	      result =
    38	        result
    39	          .where(
    40	            "uploads.retain_hours IS NULL OR uploads.created_at < current_timestamp - interval '1 hour' * uploads.retain_hours",
    41	          )
    42	          .where("uploads.created_at < ?", grace_period.hour.ago)
    43	          .where(
    44	            "((uploads.access_control_post_id IS NULL) OR (uploads.access_control_post_id IS NOT NULL AND NOT uploads.secure))",
    45	          )
    46	          .joins("LEFT JOIN upload_references ON upload_references.upload_id = uploads.id")
    47	          .where("upload_references.upload_id IS NULL")
    48	          .with_no_non_post_relations
    49	
    50	      result.find_each do |upload|
    51	        next if Upload.in_use_callbacks&.any? { |callback| callback.call(upload) }
    52	        upload.sha1.present? ? upload.destroy : upload.delete
    53	      end
    54	
    55	      ExternalUploadStub.cleanup!
    56	
    57	      self.last_cleanup = Time.zone.now.to_i
    58	    end
    59	
    60	    def last_cleanup=(timestamp)
    61	      Discourse.redis.setex(last_cleanup_key, 7.days.to_i, timestamp.to_s)
    62	    end
    63	
    64	    def last_cleanup
    65	      timestamp = Discourse.redis.get(last_cleanup_key)
    66	      timestamp ? timestamp.to_i : timestamp
    67	    end
    68	
    69	    def reset_last_cleanup!
    70	      Discourse.redis.del(last_cleanup_key)
    71	    end
    72	
    73	    protected
    74	
    75	    def last_cleanup_key
    76	      "LAST_UPLOAD_CLEANUP"
    77	    end
    78	  end
    79	end

     3	module Jobs
     4	  # Sometimes we need to update the access control metadata for a _lot_ of objects on S3 (such as when secure uploads
     5	  # is enabled), this is best spread out over many jobs instead of having to do the whole thing serially.
     6	  class SyncAccessControlForUploads < ::Jobs::Base
     7	    sidekiq_options queue: "low"
     8	
     9	    def execute(args)
    10	      return if !Discourse.store.external?
    11	      return if !args.key?(:upload_ids)
    12	
    13	      Upload
    14	        .includes(:optimized_images)
    15	        .where(id: args[:upload_ids])
    16	        .find_in_batches do |uploads|
    17	          uploads.each do |upload|
    18	            begin
    19	              Discourse.store.update_upload_access_control(upload, remove_existing_acl: true)
    20	            rescue => err
    21	              Discourse.warn_exception(
    22	                err,
    23	                message: "Failed to update upload access control",
    24	                env: {
    25	                  upload_id: upload.id,
    26	                  filename: upload.original_filename,
    27	                },
    28	              )
    29	            end
    30	          end
    31	        end
    32	    end
    33	  end
    34	end
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module Jobs
     4	  class MigrateUploadScheme < ::Jobs::Scheduled
     5	    every 10.minutes
     6	    sidekiq_options retry: false
     7	
     8	    def execute(args)
     9	      return unless SiteSetting.migrate_to_new_scheme
    10	
    11	      # clean up failed uploads
    12	      Upload
    13	        .where("created_at < ?", 1.hour.ago)
    14	        .where("LENGTH(COALESCE(url, '')) = 0")
    15	        .find_each { |upload| upload.destroy! }
    16	
    17	      # migrate uploads to new scheme
    18	      problems = Upload.migrate_to_new_scheme(limit: 50)
    19	
    20	      problems.each do |hash|
    21	        upload_id = hash[:upload].id
    22	        Discourse.handle_job_exception(
    23	          hash[:ex],
    24	          error_context(args, "Migrating upload id #{upload_id}", upload_id: upload_id),
    25	        )
    26	      end
    27	
    28	      # clean up failed optimized images
    29	      OptimizedImage
    30	        .where("LENGTH(COALESCE(url, '')) = 0")
    31	        .find_each { |optimized_image| optimized_image.destroy! }
    32	
    33	      # Clean up orphan optimized images
    34	      OptimizedImage
    35	        .joins("LEFT JOIN uploads ON optimized_images.upload_id = uploads.id")
    36	        .where("uploads.id IS NULL")
    37	        .find_each { |optimized_image| optimized_image.destroy! }
    38	
    39	      # Clean up optimized images that needs to be regenerated
    40	      OptimizedImage
    41	        .joins(:upload)
    42	        .where("optimized_images.url NOT LIKE '%/optimized/_X/%'")
    43	        .where("uploads.url LIKE '%/original/_X/%'")
    44	        .limit(50)
    45	        .find_each do |optimized_image|
    46	          upload = optimized_image.upload
    47	          optimized_image.destroy!
    48	          upload.rebake_posts_on_old_scheme
    49	        end
    50	    end
    51	  end
    52	end

     3	##
     4	# A note on determining whether an upload should be marked as secure:
     5	#
     6	# Some of these flags checked (e.g. all of the for_X flags and the opts[:type])
     7	# are only set when _initially uploading_ via UploadCreator and are not present
     8	# when an upload already exists, these will only be checked when the @creating
     9	# option is present.
    10	#
    11	# If the upload already exists the best way to figure out whether it should be
    12	# secure alongside the site settings is the access_control_post_id, because the
    13	# original post the upload is linked to has far more bearing on its security context
    14	# post-upload. If the access_control_post_id does not exist then we just rely
    15	# on the current secure? status, otherwise there would be a lot of additional
    16	# complex queries and joins to perform.
    17	#
    18	# These queries will be performed only if the @creating option is false. So if
    19	# an upload is included in a post, and it's an upload from a different source
    20	# (e.g. a category logo, site setting upload) then we will determine secure
    21	# state _based on the first place the upload was referenced_.
    22	#
    23	# NOTE: When updating this to add more cases where uploads will be marked
    24	# secure, consider uploads:secure_upload_analyse_and_update as well, which
    25	# does not use this class directly but uses an SQL version of its rules for
    26	# efficient updating of many uploads in bulk.
    27	class UploadSecurity
    28	  @@custom_public_types = []
    29	
    30	  PUBLIC_TYPES = %w[
    31	    avatar
    32	    custom_emoji
    33	    profile_background
    34	    card_background
    35	    category_logo
    36	    category_logo_dark
    37	    category_background
    38	    group_flair
    39	    badge_image
    40	    site_setting
    41	  ]
    42	
    43	  PUBLIC_UPLOAD_REFERENCE_TYPES = %w[
    44	    Badge
    45	    Category
    46	    CustomEmoji
    47	    Group
    48	    SiteSetting
    49	    ThemeField
    50	    User
    51	    UserAvatar
    52	    UserProfile
    53	  ]
    54	
    55	  def self.register_custom_public_type(type)
    56	    @@custom_public_types << type if !@@custom_public_types.include?(type)
    57	  end
    58	
    59	  # used in tests
    60	  def self.reset_custom_public_types
    61	    @@custom_public_types = []
    62	  end
    63	
    64	  def initialize(upload, opts = {})
    65	    @upload = upload
    66	    @opts = opts
    67	    @upload_type = @opts[:type]
    68	    @creating = @opts[:creating]
    69	  end
    70	
    71	  def should_be_secure?
    72	    should_be_secure_with_reason.first
    73	  end
    74	
    75	  def should_be_secure_with_reason
    76	    insecure_context_checks.each { |check, reason| return false, reason if perform_check(check) }
    77	    secure_context_checks.each do |check, reason|
    78	      return perform_check(check), reason if priority_check?(check)
    79	      return true, reason if perform_check(check)
    80	    end
    81	
    82	    [false, "no checks satisfied"]
    83	  end
    84	
    85	  private
    86	
    87	  def access_control_post
    88	    @access_control_post ||=
    89	      @upload.access_control_post_id.present? ? @upload.access_control_post : nil
    90	  end
    91	
    92	  def insecure_context_checks
    93	    {
    94	      secure_uploads_disabled: "secure uploads is disabled",
    95	      insecure_creation_for_modifiers: "one or more creation for_modifiers was satisfied",
    96	      public_type: "upload is public type",
    97	      regular_emoji: "upload is used for regular emoji",
    98	      publicly_referenced_first: "upload was publicly referenced when it was first created",
    99	    }
   100	  end
   101	
   102	  def secure_context_checks
   103	    {
   104	      login_required: "login is required",
   105	      access_control_post_should_secure_uploads: "access control post dictates security",
   106	      secure_creation_for_modifiers: "one or more creation for_modifiers was satisfied",
   107	      uploading_in_composer: "uploading via the composer",
   108	      already_secure: "upload is already secure",
   109	    }
   110	  end
   111	
   112	  # The access control check is important because that is the truest indicator
   113	  # of whether an upload should be secure or not, and thus should be returned
   114	  # immediately if there is an access control post.
   115	  def priority_check?(check)
   116	    check == :access_control_post_should_secure_uploads && access_control_post
   117	  end
   118	
   119	  def perform_check(check)
   120	    send("#{check}_check")
   121	  end
   122	
   123	  #### START PUBLIC CHECKS ####
   124	
   125	  def secure_uploads_disabled_check
   126	    !SiteSetting.secure_uploads?
   127	  end
   128	
   129	  def insecure_creation_for_modifiers_check
   130	    return false if !@creating
   131	    @upload.for_theme || @upload.for_site_setting || @upload.for_gravatar
   132	  end
   133	
   134	  def public_type_check
   135	    PUBLIC_TYPES.include?(@upload_type) || @@custom_public_types.include?(@upload_type)
   136	  end
   137	
   138	  def publicly_referenced_first_check
   139	    return false if @creating
   140	    first_reference =
   141	      @upload
   142	        .upload_references
   143	        .joins(<<~SQL)
   144	          LEFT JOIN posts ON upload_references.target_type = 'Post' AND upload_references.target_id = posts.id
   145	        SQL
   146	        .where("posts.deleted_at IS NULL")
   147	        .order("upload_references.created_at ASC, upload_references.id ASC")
   148	        .first
   149	    return false if first_reference.blank?
   150	    PUBLIC_UPLOAD_REFERENCE_TYPES.include?(first_reference.target_type)
   151	  end
   152	
   153	  def regular_emoji_check
   154	    return false if @upload.origin.blank?
   155	    uri = URI.parse(@upload.origin)
   156	    return true if Emoji.all.map(&:url).include?("#{uri.path}?#{uri.query}")
   157	    uri.path.include?("images/emoji")
   158	  end
   159	
   160	  #### END PUBLIC CHECKS ####
   161	
   162	  #--------------------------#
   163	
   164	  #### START PRIVATE CHECKS ####
   165	
   166	  def login_required_check
   167	    SiteSetting.login_required? && !SiteSetting.secure_uploads_pm_only?
   168	  end
   169	
   170	  # Whether the upload should remain secure or not after posting depends on its context,
   171	  # which is based on the post it is linked to via access_control_post_id.
   172	  #
   173	  # If that post should_secure_uploads? then the upload should also be secure.
   174	  #
   175	  # This may change to false if the upload was set to secure on upload e.g. in
   176	  # a post composer then it turned out that the post itself was not in a secure context.
   177	  #
   178	  # A post is with secure uploads if it is a private message or in a read restricted
   179	  # category. See `Post#should_secure_uploads?` for the full definition.
   180	  def access_control_post_should_secure_uploads_check
   181	    access_control_post&.should_secure_uploads?
   182	  end
   183	
   184	  def uploading_in_composer_check
   185	    @upload_type == "composer"
   186	  end
   187	
   188	  def secure_creation_for_modifiers_check
   189	    return false if !@creating
   190	    @upload.for_private_message || @upload.for_group_message
   191	  end
   192	
   193	  def already_secure_check
   194	    @upload.secure?
   195	  end
   196	
   197	  #### END PRIVATE CHECKS ####
   198	end

1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	class ThemeScreenshotsHandler
     4	  MAX_THEME_SCREENSHOT_FILE_SIZE = 1.megabyte
     5	  MAX_THEME_SCREENSHOT_DIMENSIONS = [3840, 2160] # 4K resolution
     6	  MAX_THEME_SCREENSHOT_COUNT = 2
     7	  THEME_SCREENSHOT_ALLOWED_FILE_TYPES = %w[.jpeg .jpg .png .webp].freeze
     8	
     9	  class ThemeScreenshotError < StandardError
    10	  end
    11	
    12	  def initialize(theme)
    13	    @theme = theme
    14	  end
    15	
    16	  # Screenshots here come from RemoteTheme.extract_theme_info, which
    17	  # in turn parses the theme about.json file, which is where screenshots
    18	  # are defined.
    19	  def parse_screenshots_as_theme_fields!(screenshots, theme_importer)
    20	    updated_theme_fields = []
    21	    screenshots = Array.wrap(screenshots).take(MAX_THEME_SCREENSHOT_COUNT)
    22	    screenshots.each do |relative_path|
    23	      path = theme_importer.real_path(relative_path)
    24	      next if !path.present?
    25	
    26	      screenshot_filename = File.basename(path)
    27	      screenshot_extension = File.extname(path)
    28	
    29	      if !THEME_SCREENSHOT_ALLOWED_FILE_TYPES.include?(screenshot_extension)
    30	        raise ThemeScreenshotError,
    31	              I18n.t(
    32	                "themes.import_error.screenshot_invalid_type",
    33	                file_name: screenshot_filename,
    34	                accepted_formats: THEME_SCREENSHOT_ALLOWED_FILE_TYPES.join(","),
    35	              )
    36	      end
    37	
    38	      if File.size(path) > MAX_THEME_SCREENSHOT_FILE_SIZE
    39	        raise ThemeScreenshotError,
    40	              I18n.t(
    41	                "themes.import_error.screenshot_invalid_size",
    42	                file_name: screenshot_filename,
    43	                max_size:
    44	                  ActiveSupport::NumberHelper.number_to_human_size(MAX_THEME_SCREENSHOT_FILE_SIZE),
    45	              )
    46	      end
    47	
    48	      screenshot_width, screenshot_height = FastImage.size(path)
    49	      if (screenshot_width.nil? || screenshot_height.nil?) ||
    50	           screenshot_width > MAX_THEME_SCREENSHOT_DIMENSIONS[0] ||
    51	           screenshot_height > MAX_THEME_SCREENSHOT_DIMENSIONS[1]
    52	        raise ThemeScreenshotError,
    53	              I18n.t(
    54	                "themes.import_error.screenshot_invalid_dimensions",
    55	                file_name: screenshot_filename,
    56	                width: screenshot_width.to_i,
    57	                height: screenshot_height.to_i,
    58	                max_width: MAX_THEME_SCREENSHOT_DIMENSIONS[0],
    59	                max_height: MAX_THEME_SCREENSHOT_DIMENSIONS[1],
    60	              )
    61	      end
    62	
    63	      upload =
    64	        RemoteTheme.create_upload(
    65	          theme: @theme,
    66	          path: path,
    67	          relative_path: relative_path,
    68	          skip_validations: true,
    69	        )
    70	      if !upload.errors.empty?
    71	        raise ThemeScreenshotError,
    72	              I18n.t(
    73	                "themes.import_error.screenshot",
    74	                errors: upload.errors.full_messages.join(","),
    75	              )
    76	      end
    77	
    78	      screenshot_filename_clean =
    79	        FileHelper.sanitize_filename(screenshot_filename.gsub(screenshot_extension, ""))
    80	      updated_theme_fields << @theme.set_field(
    81	        target: :common,
    82	        name: "screenshot_#{screenshot_filename_clean}",
    83	        type: :theme_screenshot_upload_var,
    84	        upload_id: upload.id,
    85	      )
    86	    end
    87	    updated_theme_fields
    88	  end
    89	end
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	class ThemeSettingsManager::Upload < ThemeSettingsManager
     4	  def value
     5	    has_record? ? cdn_url(db_record.value) : default
     6	  end
     7	
     8	  def default
     9	    upload_id = default_upload_id
    10	    return if upload_id.blank?
    11	
    12	    cdn_url(upload_id)
    13	  end
    14	
    15	  def value=(new_value)
    16	    if new_value.present?
    17	      if new_value == default
    18	        new_value = default_upload_id
    19	      else
    20	        upload = ::Upload.find_by(url: new_value)
    21	        new_value = upload.id if upload.present?
    22	      end
    23	    end
    24	
    25	    super(new_value)
    26	  end
    27	
    28	  private
    29	
    30	  def cdn_url(upload_id)
    31	    return if upload_id.blank?
    32	
    33	    upload = ::Upload.find_by_id(upload_id.to_i)
    34	    return if upload.blank?
    35	
    36	    Discourse.store.cdn_url(upload.url)
    37	  end
    38	
    39	  def default_upload_id
    40	    theme_field =
    41	      theme.theme_fields.find_by(name: @default, type_id: ThemeField.types[:theme_upload_var])
    42	    return if theme_field.blank?
    43	
    44	    theme_field.upload_id
    45	  end
    46	end

   151	            )
   152	          rescue AssetProcessor::TranspileError => e
   153	            message = "[THEME #{theme_id} '#{theme.name}'] Compile error: #{e.message}"
   154	            errors << message
   155	            { "code" => "console.error(#{message.to_json});\n", "map" => nil }

   163	      end

   171	
   172	  def validate_svg_sprite_xml
   173	    upload = Upload.find_by(id: self.upload_id)
   174	
   175	    return "Error with #{self.name}: No upload found for the provided upload_id" if upload.nil?
   176	
   177	    if Discourse.store.external?
   178	      path = Discourse.store.download(upload)
   179	    else
   180	      path = Discourse.store.path_for(upload)
   181	    end
   182	
   183	    return "Error with #{self.name}: Could not download icon sprite" if path.nil?
   184	
   185	    error = nil
   186	
   187	    begin
   188	      content = File.read(path)
   189	      if content.to_s.bytesize > SvgSprite::MAX_THEME_SPRITE_SIZE
   190	        error = "Error with #{self.name}: Icon sprite file is too large"
   191	      else
   192	        Nokogiri.XML(content) { |config| config.options = Nokogiri::XML::ParseOptions::NOBLANKS }
   193	      end
   194	    rescue => e
   195	      error = "Error with #{self.name}: #{e.inspect}"
   196	    end
   197	
   198	    error
   199	  end
   200	
   201	  def raw_translation_data(internal: false)
   202	    # Might raise ThemeTranslationParser::InvalidYaml
   203	    ThemeTranslationParser.new(self, internal: internal).load
   204	  end
   205	
   206	  def translation_data(with_overrides: true, internal: false, fallback_fields: nil)
   207	    fallback_fields ||= theme.theme_fields.filter_locale_fields(I18n.fallbacks[name])
   208	
   209	    fallback_data =
   210	      fallback_fields.each_with_index.map do |field, index|
   211	        begin
   212	          field.raw_translation_data(internal: internal)
   213	        rescue ThemeTranslationParser::InvalidYaml
   214	          # If this is the locale with the error, raise it.
   215	          # If not, let the other theme_field raise the error when it processes itself
   216	          raise if field.id == id
   217	          {}
   218	        end
   219	      end

  1362	    upload_ids =
  1363	      SchemaSettingsObjectValidator.property_values_of_type(
  1364	        schema: schema,
  1365	        objects: objects,
  1366	        type: "upload",
  1367	      )
  1368	
  1369	    uploads_by_id = Upload.where(id: upload_ids).index_by(&:id)
  1370	    objects.map { |obj| hydrate_uploads_in_object(obj, schema[:properties], uploads_by_id) }
  1371	  end
  1372	
  1373	  def hydrate_uploads_in_object(object, properties, uploads_by_id)
  1374	    properties.each do |prop_key, prop_value|
  1375	      case prop_value[:type]
  1376	      when "upload"
  1377	        key = prop_key.to_s
  1378	        upload_id = object[key]
  1379	        upload = uploads_by_id[upload_id]
  1380	        object[key] = upload.url if upload
  1381	      when "objects"
  1382	        nested_objects = object[prop_key.to_s]
  1383	        if nested_objects.is_a?(Array)
  1384	          nested_objects.each do |nested_obj|
  1385	            hydrate_uploads_in_object(nested_obj, prop_value[:schema][:properties], uploads_by_id)
  1386	          end
  1387	        end
  1388	      end
  1389	    end
  1390	
  1391	    object
  1392	  end
  1393	end

    20	
    21	      self.settings =
    22	        self
    23	          .settings
    24	          .to_a
    25	          .map do |setting|
    26	            Setting.new(

    28	              setting[:value].to_s.strip,
    29	              !!setting[:backfill],

    40	        self.settings.map do |setting|
    41	          raw_value = setting.value
    42	
    43	          setting.value =
    44	            case SiteSetting.type_supervisor.get_type(setting.name)
    45	            when :integer
    46	              raw_value.tr("^-0-9", "").to_i
    47	            when :file_size_restriction
    48	              raw_value.tr("^0-9", "").to_i
    49	            when :uploaded_image_list
    50	              raw_value.blank? ? "" : Upload.get_from_urls(raw_value.split("|")).to_a
    51	            when :upload
    52	              Upload.get_from_url(raw_value) || ""
    53	            else
    54	              raw_value
    55	            end
    56	
    57	          # Make sure to get the correct value here based on type, otherwise
    58	          # we can end up inserting a staff action log when e.g. setting the value
    59	          # to "false" when the setting value is already false.
    60	          setting.value = SiteSetting.type_supervisor.to_rb_value(setting.name, setting.value)
    61	
    62	          setting
    63	        end
    64	    end
    65	  end

    70	  policy :settings_are_visible, class_name: SiteSetting::Policy::SettingsAreVisible
    71	  policy :settings_are_configurable, class_name: SiteSetting::Policy::SettingsAreConfigurable

    73	  try do

    86	  def save(params:, options:, guardian:)
    87	    params.settings.each do |setting|
    88	      detailed_message =
    89	        if default_user_preference?(setting)
    90	          value =
    91	            setting.backfill ? I18n.t("csv_export.boolean_yes") : I18n.t("csv_export.boolean_no")
    92	          I18n.t("staff_action_logs.site_setting.update_existing_users", value:)
    93	        end
    94	
    95	      setting.change =
    96	        SiteSetting.set_and_log(
    97	          options.overridden_setting_names[setting.name] || setting.name,
    98	          setting.value,
    99	          guardian.user,
   100	          detailed_message,
   101	        )
   102	    end

   104	
   105	  def backfill(params:)
   106	    params.settings.each do |setting|
   107	      next if !setting.backfill || !default_user_preference?(setting)
   108	
   109	      SiteSettingUpdateExistingUsers.call(
   110	        setting.name.to_s,
   111	        setting.change.new_value,
   112	        setting.change.previous_value,
   113	      )
   114	    end
   115	  end
   116	
   117	  def default_user_preference?(setting)
   118	    SiteSetting::DEFAULT_USER_PREFERENCES.include?(setting.name.to_s)

     3	class SiteSetting::SplashScreenImageChanged
     4	  include Service::Base
     5	
     6	  params { attribute :upload_id, :integer }
     7	
     8	  model :upload
     9	  model :svg
    10	  model :cleaned_svg
    11	  step :save_cleaned_svg_upload
    12	  step :clear_cache
    13	
    14	  private
    15	
    16	  def fetch_upload(params:)
    17	    Upload.find_by(id: params.upload_id)
    18	  end
    19	
    20	  def fetch_svg(upload:)
    21	    content =
    22	      begin
    23	        upload.content
    24	      rescue StandardError
    25	        nil
    26	      end
    27	
    28	    return nil if content.blank?
    29	
    30	    Nokogiri.XML(content).at_css("svg")
    31	  end
    32	
    33	  def fetch_cleaned_svg(svg:)
    34	    # Disables SMIL animations which can cause performance issues,
    35	    # CSS animations are preferred.
    36	    svg.xpath(
    37	      ".//*[local-name()='animate' or local-name()='animateTransform' or local-name()='animateMotion' or local-name()='set']",
    38	    ).each(&:remove)
    39	
    40	    # Remove explicit dimensions so the SVG scales via viewBox
    41	    if svg["viewBox"].present?
    42	      svg.remove_attribute("width")
    43	      svg.remove_attribute("height")
    44	    end
    45	
    46	    svg.to_xml
    47	  end
    48	
    49	  def save_cleaned_svg_upload(cleaned_svg:, upload:)
    50	    return if cleaned_svg == upload.content
    51	
    52	    Tempfile.open(%w[splash_screen .svg]) do |tmp|
    53	      tmp.write(cleaned_svg)
    54	      tmp.rewind
    55	
    56	      new_sha1 = Upload.generate_digest(tmp.path)
    57	      existing = Upload.find_by(sha1: new_sha1)
    58	
    59	      if existing && existing.id != upload.id
    60	        SiteSetting.splash_screen_image = existing.id
    61	      else
    62	        old_path = Discourse.store.get_path_for_upload(upload)
    63	        old_url = upload.url
    64	        upload.tap do |u|
    65	          u.sha1 = new_sha1
    66	          u.filesize = tmp.size
    67	          u.url = Discourse.store.store_upload(tmp, u)
    68	          u.save!(validate: false)
    69	        end
    70	
    71	        Discourse.store.remove_file(old_url, old_path) if upload.url != old_url
    72	      end
    73	    end
    74	  end
    75	
    76	  def clear_cache(upload:)
    77	    Discourse.cache.delete("splash_screen_svg_#{upload.id}_#{upload.sha1}")
    78	  end
    79	end

373	    def process_upload_group(upload_group)
   374	      primary = upload_group.first
   375	      primary_filename = upload_path_in_archive(primary)
   376	
   377	      return if !download_upload_to_file(primary, primary_filename)
   378	
   379	      upload_group
   380	        .drop(1)
   381	        .each do |duplicate|
   382	          duplicate_filename = upload_path_in_archive(duplicate)
   383	          primary_filename = create_hardlink(primary_filename, duplicate, duplicate_filename)
   384	        end
   385	    end
   386	
   387	    def upload_path_in_archive(upload_data)
   388	      path = @s3_store.get_path_for_upload(upload_data)
   389	      File.join(@tmp_directory, @upload_directory, path)
   390	    end
   391	
   392	    def download_upload_to_file(upload_data, filename)
   393	      FileUtils.mkdir_p(File.dirname(filename))
   394	      @s3_store.download_file(upload_data, filename)
   395	      increment_and_log_progress(:downloaded)
   396	      true
   397	    rescue StandardError => ex
   398	      log "Failed to download file with upload ID #{upload_data.id} from S3", ex
   399	      false
   400	    end
   401	
   402	    def create_hardlink(source_filename, upload_data, target_filename)
   403	      FileUtils.mkdir_p(File.dirname(target_filename))
   404	      FileUtils.ln(source_filename, target_filename)
   405	      increment_and_log_progress(:hardlinked)
   406	      source_filename
   407	    rescue Errno::EMLINK
   408	      # Filesystem hardlink limit reached - copy file and use as new primary
   409	      FileUtils.cp(source_filename, target_filename)
   410	      increment_and_log_progress(:copied)
   411	      target_filename
   412	    rescue StandardError => ex
   413	      log "Failed to create hardlink for upload ID #{upload_data.id}, copying instead", ex
   414	      FileUtils.cp(source_filename, target_filename)
   415	      increment_and_log_progress(:copied)
   416	      source_filename
   417	    end
   418	
   419	    def increment_and_log_progress(type)
   420	      @download_stats[type] += 1
   421	      total = @download_stats[:downloaded] + @download_stats[:hardlinked] + @download_stats[:copied]
   422	      return if total % 1000 != 0
   423	
   424	      log "#{total} files processed (#{@download_stats[:downloaded]} downloaded, #{@download_stats[:hardlinked]} hardlinked, #{@download_stats[:copied]} copied). Still processing..."
   425	    end
   426	
   427	    def upload_archive
   428	      return unless store.remote?
   429	
   430	      log "Uploading archive..."
   431	      content_type =
   432	        MiniMime.lookup_by_filename(@backup_filename)&.content_type || "application/gzip"
   433	      archive_path = File.join(@archive_directory, @backup_filename)
   434	      store.upload_file(@backup_filename, archive_path, content_type)
   435	    end
   436	
   437	    def after_create_hook
   438	      log "Executing the after_create_hook for the backup..."
   439	      DiscourseEvent.trigger(:backup_created)
   440	    end
   441	
   442	    def delete_old
   443	      return if Rails.env.development?
   444	
   445	      log "Deleting old backups..."
   446	      store.delete_old
   447	    rescue => ex
   448	      log "Something went wrong while deleting old backups.", ex
   449	    end
   450	
   451	    def notify_user
   452	      return if success && @user.id == Discourse::SYSTEM_USER_ID
   453	
   454	      log "Notifying '#{@user.username}' of the end of the backup..."
   455	      status = success ? :backup_succeeded : :backup_failed
   456	
   457	      logs = Discourse::Utils.logs_markdown(@logs, user: @user)
   458	      post = SystemMessage.create_from_system_user(@user, status, logs: logs)
   459	
   460	      post.topic.invite_group(@user, Group[:admins]) if @user.id == Discourse::SYSTEM_USER_ID
   461	    rescue => ex
   462	      log "Something went wrong while notifying user.", ex
   463	    end
   464	
   465	    def clean_up
   466	      log "Cleaning stuff up..."
   467	      delete_uploaded_archive
   468	      remove_tar_leftovers
   469	      mark_backup_as_not_running
   470	      refresh_disk_space if success

  1100	  def self.find_missing_uploads(include_local_upload: true)
  1101	    missing_uploads = []
  1102	    missing_post_uploads = {}
  1103	    count = 0
  1104	
  1105	    DistributedMutex.synchronize("find_missing_uploads", validity: 30.minutes) do
  1106	      PostCustomField.where(name: Post::MISSING_UPLOADS).delete_all
  1107	
  1108	      query =
  1109	        Post
  1110	          .have_uploads
  1111	          .joins(:topic)
  1112	          .joins(
  1113	            "LEFT JOIN post_custom_fields ON posts.id = post_custom_fields.post_id AND post_custom_fields.name = '#{Post::MISSING_UPLOADS_IGNORED}'",
  1114	          )
  1115	          .where("post_custom_fields.id IS NULL")
  1116	          .select(:id, :cooked)
  1117	
  1118	      query.find_in_batches do |posts|
  1119	        ids = posts.pluck(:id)
  1120	        sha1s =
  1121	          Upload
  1122	            .joins(:upload_references)
  1123	            .where(upload_references: { target_type: "Post" })
  1124	            .where("upload_references.target_id BETWEEN ? AND ?", ids.min, ids.max)
  1125	            .pluck(:sha1)
  1126	
  1127	        posts.each do |post|
  1128	          post.each_upload_url do |src, path, sha1|
  1129	            next if sha1.present? && sha1s.include?(sha1)
  1130	
  1131	            missing_post_uploads[post.id] ||= []
  1132	
  1133	            if missing_uploads.include?(src)
  1134	              missing_post_uploads[post.id] << src
  1135	              next
  1136	            end
  1137	
  1138	            upload_id = nil
  1139	            upload_id = Upload.where(sha1: sha1).pick(:id) if sha1.present?
  1140	            upload_id ||= yield(post, src, path, sha1)
  1141	
  1142	            if upload_id.blank?
  1143	              missing_uploads << src
  1144	              missing_post_uploads[post.id] << src
  1145	            end
  1146	          end
  1147	        end
  1148	      end
  1149	
  1150	      missing_post_uploads =
  1151	        missing_post_uploads.reject do |post_id, uploads|
  1152	          if uploads.present?
  1153	            PostCustomField.create!(
  1154	              post_id: post_id,
  1155	              name: Post::MISSING_UPLOADS,
  1156	              value: uploads.to_json,
  1157	            )
  1158	            count += uploads.count
  1159	          end
  1160	
  1161	          uploads.empty?
  1162	        end
  1163	    end
  1164	
  1165	    { uploads: missing_uploads, post_uploads: missing_post_uploads, count: count }
  1166	  end

   685	        category_id: include_subcategories ? Category.subcategory_ids(category_id) : category_id,
   686	      ) if category_id
   687	
   688	    if group_ids
   689	      result =
   690	        result
   691	          .joins("INNER JOIN users ON users.id = topics.user_id")
   692	          .joins("INNER JOIN group_users ON group_users.user_id = users.id")
   693	          .where("group_users.group_id IN (?)", group_ids)
   694	    end
   695	
   696	    result.count
   697	  end
   698	
   699	  def private_message?
   700	    self.archetype == Archetype.private_message
   701	  end
   702	
   703	  def regular?
   704	    self.archetype == Archetype.default
   705	  end
   706	
   707	  def open?
   708	    !self.closed?
   709	  end
   710	
   711	  MAX_SIMILAR_BODY_LENGTH = 200
   712	  SIMILAR_TOPIC_SEARCH_LIMIT = 10
   713	  SIMILAR_TOPIC_LIMIT = 3
   714	  SIMILAR_TOPIC_MAX_BLURB_LENGTH = 500
   715	
   716	  def self.similar_to(title, raw, user = nil)
   717	    return [] if title.blank?
   718	
   719	    raw = raw.presence || ""
   720	    search_data = Search.prepare_data(title.strip)

     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	# This model indicates an 'attempt' to create a topic thumbnail
     4	# for an upload. This means we don't keep trying to create optimized
     5	# images for small/invalid original images.
     6	#
     7	# Foreign keys with ON DELETE CASCADE are used to ensure unneeded data
     8	# is deleted automatically
     9	class TopicThumbnail < ActiveRecord::Base
    10	  belongs_to :upload
    11	  belongs_to :optimized_image
    12	
    13	  def self.find_or_create_for!(original, max_width:, max_height:)
    14	    existing =
    15	      TopicThumbnail.find_by(upload: original, max_width: max_width, max_height: max_height)
    16	    return existing if existing
    17	    return nil if !SiteSetting.create_thumbnails?
    18	
    19	    target_width, target_height =
    20	      ImageSizer.resize(
    21	        original.width,
    22	        original.height,
    23	        { max_width: max_width, max_height: max_height },
    24	      )
    25	
    26	    needs_optimization = target_width < original.width && target_height < original.height
    27	    skip_animated = SiteSetting.animated_topic_thumbnails && original.animated?
    28	
    29	    if needs_optimization && !skip_animated
    30	      optimized = OptimizedImage.create_for(original, target_width, target_height)
    31	    end
    32	
    33	    # may have been associated already, bulk insert will skip dupes
    34	    TopicThumbnail.insert_all(
    35	      [
    36	        upload_id: original.id,
    37	        max_width: max_width,
    38	        max_height: max_height,
    39	        optimized_image_id: optimized&.id,
    40	      ],
    41	    )
    42	
    43	    TopicThumbnail.find_by(upload: original, max_width: max_width, max_height: max_height)
    44	  end
    45	
    46	  def self.ensure_consistency!
    47	    # Clean up records for broken upload links or broken optimized image links
    48	    TopicThumbnail
    49	      .joins("LEFT JOIN uploads on upload_id = uploads.id")
    50	      .joins("LEFT JOIN optimized_images on optimized_image_id = optimized_images.id")
    51	      .where(<<~SQL)
    52	        (optimized_image_id IS NOT NULL AND optimized_images IS NULL)
    53	        OR uploads IS NULL
    54	      SQL
    55	      .delete_all
    56	
    57	    # Delete records for sizes which are no longer needed
    58	    sizes =
    59	      Topic.thumbnail_sizes +
    60	        ThemeModifierHelper.new(theme_ids: Theme.pluck(:id)).topic_thumbnail_sizes
    61	    sizes_sql =
    62	      sizes.map { |s| "(max_width = #{s[0].to_i} AND max_height = #{s[1].to_i})" }.join(" OR ")
    63	    TopicThumbnail.where.not(sizes_sql).delete_all
    64	  end
    65	end

     3	class ThemeSetting < ActiveRecord::Base
     4	  belongs_to :theme
     5	
     6	  has_many :upload_references, as: :target, dependent: :destroy
     7	
     8	  TYPES_ENUM =
     9	    Enum.new(integer: 0, float: 1, string: 2, bool: 3, list: 4, enum: 5, upload: 6, objects: 7)
    10	
    11	  MAXIMUM_JSON_VALUE_SIZE_BYTES = 0.5 * 1024 * 1024 # 0.5 MB
    12	
    13	  validates :name, :theme, presence: true
    14	  validates :data_type, inclusion: { in: TYPES_ENUM.values }
    15	  validate :json_value_size, if: -> { self.data_type == TYPES_ENUM[:objects] }
    16	  validates :name, length: { maximum: 255 }
    17	
    18	  after_destroy :clear_settings_cache
    19	  after_save :clear_settings_cache
    20	
    21	  after_save do
    22	    if self.data_type == ThemeSetting.types[:upload] && saved_change_to_value?
    23	      UploadReference.ensure_exist!(upload_ids: [self.value], target: self)
    24	    elsif self.data_type == ThemeSetting.types[:objects] && saved_change_to_json_value? &&
    25	          self.json_value.present?
    26	      upload_values =
    27	        SchemaSettingsObjectValidator.property_values_of_type(
    28	          schema: theme.settings[self.name.to_sym].schema,
    29	          objects: self.json_value,
    30	          type: "upload",
    31	        )
    32	
    33	      # Convert URLs to upload IDs (values can be either integer IDs or URL strings)
    34	      upload_ids =
    35	        upload_values.filter_map do |value|
    36	          if value.is_a?(Integer)
    37	            value
    38	          elsif value.is_a?(String) && value.present?
    39	            Upload.get_from_url(value)&.id
    40	          end
    41	        end
    42	
    43	      # Always call ensure_exist! to clean up old references when uploads are removed
    44	      UploadReference.ensure_exist!(upload_ids: upload_ids, target: self)
    45	    end
    46	
    47	    if theme.theme_modifier_set.refresh_theme_setting_modifiers(
    48	         target_setting_name: self.name,
    49	         target_setting_value: self.value,
    50	       )
    51	      theme.theme_modifier_set.save!
    52	    end
    53	  end
    54	
    55	  def clear_settings_cache
    56	    # All necessary caches will be cleared on next ensure_baked!
    57	    theme.settings_field&.invalidate_baked!
    58	  end

    61	    TYPES_ENUM
    62	  end

    66	    when Integer
    67	      types[:integer]
    68	    when Float
    69	      types[:float]
    70	    when String

    41	  def build_combined_actions(actions, guardian, args)
    42	    unless approved?
    43	      if topic&.closed?
    44	        build_action(actions, :approve_post_closed, icon: "check", confirm: true)
    45	      else
    46	        build_action(actions, :approve_post, icon: "check") if target_created_by.present?
    47	      end
    48	    end
    49	
    50	    if pending?
    51	      reject_bundle =
    52	        actions.add_bundle(
    53	          "#{id}-reject-post",
    54	          label: "reviewables.actions.reject_post_bundle.title",
    55	        )
    56	
    57	      build_action(actions, :reject_post, bundle: reject_bundle, icon: "xmark")
    58	      build_action(actions, :revise_and_reject_post, bundle: reject_bundle, icon: "envelope")
    59	
    60	      delete_user_actions(actions, reject_bundle) if guardian.can_delete_user?(target_created_by)

    62	
    63	    build_action(actions, :delete) if guardian.can_delete?(self)
    64	  end
    65	
    66	  def build_editable_fields(fields, guardian, args)
    67	    if pending?
    68	      # We can edit category / title if it's a new topic
    69	      if topic_id.blank?

    74	        fields.add("category_id", :category) if guardian.is_staff?
    75	
    76	        fields.add("payload.tags", :tags)
    77	      end
    78	
    79	      fields.add("payload.raw", :editor)
    80	    end

     3	module GlobalPath
     4	  def path(p)
     5	    "#{GlobalSetting.relative_url_root}#{p}"
     6	  end
     7	
     8	  def cdn_path(p)
     9	    GlobalSetting.cdn_url.blank? ? p : "#{GlobalSetting.cdn_url}#{path(p)}"
    10	  end
    11	
    12	  def upload_cdn_path(p)
    13	    p = Discourse.store.cdn_url(p) if SiteSetting.Upload.s3_cdn_url.present?
    14	
    15	    (p =~ /\Ahttp/ || p =~ %r{\A//}) ? p : cdn_path(p)
    16	  end
    17	
    18	  def cdn_relative_path(path)
    19	    if (cdn_url = GlobalSetting.cdn_url).present?
    20	      URI.parse(cdn_url).path + path
    21	    else
    22	      path
    23	    end
    24	  end
    25	
    26	  def full_cdn_url(url)
    27	    uri = URI.parse(UrlHelper.absolute(upload_cdn_path(url)))
    28	    uri.scheme = SiteSetting.scheme if uri.scheme.blank?
    29	    uri.to_s
    30	  end
    31	
    32	  extend self
    33	end

     6	  def validate(upload)
     7	    # staff can upload any file in PM
     8	    if (upload.for_private_message && SiteSetting.allow_staff_to_upload_any_file_in_pm)
     9	      return true if upload.user&.staff?
    10	    end
    11	
    12	    # check the attachment blocklist
    13	    if upload.for_group_message && SiteSetting.allow_all_attachments_for_group_messages
    14	      return upload.original_filename =~ SiteSetting.blocked_attachment_filenames_regex
    15	    end
    16	
    17	    extension = File.extname(upload.original_filename)[1..-1] || ""
    18	
    19	    return validate_site_setting_upload(upload, extension) if upload.for_site_setting
    20	
    21	    if upload.for_gravatar && FileHelper.supported_gravatar_extensions.include?(extension)
    22	      maximum_image_file_size(upload)
    23	      return true
    24	    end
    25	
    26	    return true if changing_upload_security?(upload)
    27	
    28	    if is_authorized?(upload, extension)
    29	      if FileHelper.is_supported_image?(upload.original_filename)
    30	        authorized_image_extension(upload, extension)
    31	        maximum_image_file_size(upload)
    32	      else
    33	        authorized_attachment_extension(upload, extension)
    34	        maximum_attachment_file_size(upload)
    35	      end
    36	    end
    37	  end
    38	
    39	  def validate_site_setting_upload(upload, extension)
    40	    unless upload.user&.staff?
    41	      upload.errors.add(:base, I18n.t("upload.unauthorized"))
    42	      return false
    43	    end
    44	
    45	    setting_opts = site_setting_type_hash(upload.site_setting_name)
    46	    authorized_extensions = setting_opts[:authorized_extensions]
    47	
    48	    if authorized_extensions.present?
    49	      unless extension_allowed_for_site_setting?(extension, authorized_extensions)
    50	        upload.errors.add(
    51	          :original_filename,
    52	          I18n.t("upload.unauthorized", authorized_extensions: authorized_extensions),
    53	        )
    54	        return false
    55	      end
    56	
    57	      validate_site_setting_file_size(upload, setting_opts)
    58	    else
    59	      unless FileHelper.is_supported_image?(upload.original_filename)
    60	        upload.errors.add(:original_filename, I18n.t("upload.images_only"))
    61	        return false
    62	      end
    63	
    64	      validate_site_setting_file_size(upload, setting_opts)
    65	    end
    66	
    67	    upload.errors.empty?

   100	  private
   101	
   102	  def site_setting_type_hash(setting_name)
   103	    return {} if setting_name.blank?
   104	    SiteSetting.type_supervisor.type_hash(setting_name.to_sym)
   105	  end
   106	
   107	  def validate_site_setting_file_size(upload, setting_opts)
   108	    return if !upload.validate_file_size
   109	
   110	    max_size_kb = setting_opts[:max_file_size_kb]
   111	
   112	    if max_size_kb.present?
   113	      max_size_bytes = max_size_kb.to_i.kilobytes
   114	      if upload.filesize > max_size_bytes
   115	        upload.errors.add(
   116	          :filesize,
   117	          I18n.t(
   118	            "upload.attachments.too_large_humanized",
   119	            max_size: ActiveSupport::NumberHelper.number_to_human_size(max_size_bytes),
   120	          ),
   121	        )
   122	      end
   123	    elsif FileHelper.is_supported_image?(upload.original_filename)
   124	      maximum_image_file_size(upload)
   125	    else
   126	      maximum_attachment_file_size(upload)
   127	    end
   128	  end
   129	
   130	  def extension_allowed_for_site_setting?(extension, authorized_extensions)
   131	    extensions_to_set(authorized_extensions).include?(extension.downcase)
   132	  end
   133	
   134	  def extensions_to_set(exts)
   135	    extensions = Set.new
   136	
   137	    exts
   138	      .gsub(/[\s\.]+/, "")
   139	      .downcase
   140	      .split("|")
   141	      .each { |extension| extensions << extension if extension.exclude?("*") }
   142	
   143	    extensions
   144	  end
   145	
   146	  def authorized_extensions(upload)
   147	    extensions =
   148	      if upload.for_theme
   149	        SiteSetting.theme_authorized_extensions
   150	      elsif upload.for_export
   151	        SiteSetting.export_authorized_extensions
   152	      else
   153	        SiteSetting.authorized_extensions
   154	      end
   155	    extensions_to_set(extensions)
   156	  end
   157	
   158	  def authorized_images(upload)
   159	    authorized_extensions(upload) & FileHelper.supported_images
   160	  end
   161	
   162	  def authorized_attachments(upload)
   163	    authorized_extensions(upload) - FileHelper.supported_images
   164	  end
   165	
   166	  def authorizes_all_extensions?(upload)
   167	    if upload.user&.staff?
   168	      return true if SiteSetting.authorized_extensions_for_staff.include?("*")
   169	    end
   170	    extensions =
   171	      if upload.for_theme
   172	        SiteSetting.theme_authorized_extensions
   173	      elsif upload.for_export
   174	        SiteSetting.export_authorized_extensions
   175	      else
   176	        SiteSetting.authorized_extensions
   177	      end
   178	    extensions.include?("*")
   179	  end
   180	
   181	  def extension_authorized?(upload, extension, extensions)
   182	    return true if authorizes_all_extensions?(upload)
   183	
   184	    staff_extensions = Set.new
   185	    if upload.user&.staff?
   186	      staff_extensions = extensions_to_set(SiteSetting.authorized_extensions_for_staff)
   187	      return true if staff_extensions.include?(extension.downcase)
   188	    end
   189	
   190	    unless authorized = extensions.include?(extension.downcase)
   191	      message =
   192	        I18n.t(
   193	          "upload.unauthorized",
   194	          authorized_extensions: (extensions | staff_extensions).to_a.join(", "),
   195	        )
   196	      upload.errors.add(:original_filename, message)
   197	    end
   198	
   199	    authorized
   200	  end
   201	
   202	  def maximum_file_size(upload, type)
   203	    return if !upload.validate_file_size
   204	
   205	    max_size_kb =
   206	      if upload.for_export
   207	        SiteSetting.max_export_file_size_kb
   208	      else
   209	        if upload.user&.id == Discourse::SYSTEM_USER_ID && type == "attachment"
   210	          [
   211	            SiteSetting.get("system_user_max_attachment_size_kb"),
   212	            SiteSetting.get("max_attachment_size_kb"),
   213	          ].max
   214	        else
   215	          SiteSetting.get("max_#{type}_size_kb")
   216	        end
   217	      end
   218	
   219	    max_size_bytes = max_size_kb.kilobytes
   220	
   221	    if upload.filesize > max_size_bytes
   222	      message =
   223	        I18n.t(
   224	          "upload.#{type}s.too_large_humanized",
   225	          max_size: ActiveSupport::NumberHelper.number_to_human_size(max_size_bytes),
   226	        )
   227	      upload.errors.add(:filesize, message)
   228	    end
   229	  end
   230	end

     3	module Chat
     4	  class MessageProcessor
     5	    include ::CookedProcessorMixin
     6	    IMG_FILETYPES = %w[jpg jpeg gif png heic heif webp]
     7	
     8	    def initialize(chat_message, opts = {})
     9	      @model = chat_message
    10	      @previous_cooked = (chat_message.cooked || "").dup
    11	      @should_secure_uploads = false
    12	      @size_cache = {}
    13	      @opts = opts
    14	
    15	      cook_opts = { user_id: chat_message.last_editor_id }
    16	      cook_opts[:invalidate_oneboxes] = true if opts[:invalidate_oneboxes] == true
    17	      cooked = Chat::Message.cook(chat_message.message, **cook_opts)
    18	      @doc = Loofah.html5_fragment(cooked)
    19	    end
    20	
    21	    def run!
    22	      post_process_oneboxes
    23	      process_thumbnails
    24	      post_process_videos
    25	      add_lightbox_to_images
    26	      DiscourseEvent.trigger(:chat_message_processed, @doc, @model)
    27	    end
    28	
    29	    def process_thumbnails
    30	      return if !SiteSetting.create_thumbnails
    31	
    32	      @model.uploads.each do |upload|
    33	        next if upload.blank? || IMG_FILETYPES.exclude?(upload.extension&.downcase)
    34	
    35	        if upload.width <= SiteSetting.max_image_width &&
    36	             upload.height <= SiteSetting.max_image_height
    37	          return false
    38	        end
    39	
    40	        crop =
    41	          SiteSetting.min_ratio_to_crop > 0 &&
    42	            upload.width.to_f / upload.height.to_f < SiteSetting.min_ratio_to_crop
    43	
    44	        width = upload.thumbnail_width
    45	        height = upload.thumbnail_height
    46	
    47	        # create the main thumbnail
    48	        upload.create_thumbnail!(width, height, crop: crop)
    49	
    50	        # create additional responsive thumbnails
    51	        each_responsive_ratio do |ratio|
    52	          resized_w = (width * ratio).to_i
    53	          resized_h = (height * ratio).to_i
    54	
    55	          if upload.width && resized_w <= upload.width
    56	            upload.create_thumbnail!(resized_w, resized_h, crop: crop)
    57	          end
    58	        end
    59	      end
    60	    end
    61	
    62	    def add_lightbox_to_images
    63	      @doc
    64	        .css("img")
    65	        .each do |img|
    66	          if img["class"]&.include?("emoji") || img["class"]&.include?("avatar") ||
    67	               img["data-base62-sha1"].blank?
    68	            next
    69	          end
    70	
    71	          sha1 = Upload.sha1_from_base62_encoded(img["data-base62-sha1"])
    72	          if upload = Upload.find_by(sha1: sha1)
    73	            img["data-large-src"] = UrlHelper.cook_url(upload.url, secure: upload.secure?)
    74	            img["data-download-href"] = upload.short_path
    75	            img["data-target-width"] = upload.width
    76	            img["data-target-height"] = upload.height
    77	            img["class"] = "#{img["class"]} lightbox".strip
    78	          end
    79	        end
    80	    end
    81

    83	      []
    84	    end
    85	
    86	    def broken_images
    87	      []
    88	    end
    89	
    90	    def downloaded_images
    91	      {}
    92	    end
    93	
    94	    def post_process_videos
    95	      changes_made = false
    96	
    97	      begin
    98	        @doc
    99	          .css(".video-placeholder-container")
   100	          .each do |container|
   101	            src = container["data-video-src"]
   102	            next if src.blank?
   103	
   104	            # Look for optimized video
   105	            upload = Upload.get_from_url(src)
   106	            if upload && optimized_video = OptimizedVideo.find_by(upload_id: upload.id)
   107	              optimized_url = Discourse.store.cdn_url(optimized_video.optimized_upload.url)
   108	              # Only update if the URL is different
   109	              if container["data-video-src"] != optimized_url
   110	                container["data-original-video-src"] = container["data-video-src"] unless container[
   111	                  "data-original-video-src"
   112	                ]
   113	                container["data-video-src"] = optimized_url
   114	                changes_made = true
   115	              end
   116	              # Ensure we maintain reference to original upload and optimized upload
   117	              UploadReference.ensure_exist!(
   118	                upload_ids: [upload.id, optimized_video.optimized_upload.id],
   119	                target: @model,
   120	              )
   121	            end
   122	          end
   123	
   124	        # Handle video tags with source elements (for direct video uploads in cooked HTML)
   125	        @doc
   126	          .css("video.chat-video-upload source, video source")
   127	          .each do |source|
   128	            src = source["src"]
   129	            next if src.blank?
   130	
   131	            # Look for optimized video
   132	            upload = Upload.get_from_url(src)
   133	            if upload && optimized_video = OptimizedVideo.find_by(upload_id: upload.id)
   134	              optimized_url = Discourse.store.cdn_url(optimized_video.optimized_upload.url)
   135	              # Only update if the URL is different
   136	              if source["src"] != optimized_url
   137	                source["data-original-src"] = source["src"] unless source["data-original-src"]
   138	                source["src"] = optimized_url
   139	                changes_made = true
   140	              end
   141	              # Ensure we maintain reference to original upload and optimized upload
   142	              UploadReference.ensure_exist!(
   143	                upload_ids: [upload.id, optimized_video.optimized_upload.id],
   144	                target: @model,
   145	              )
   146	            end
   147	          end
   148	
   149	        # Also check uploads directly associated with the message
   150	        @model.uploads.each do |upload|
   151	          next unless FileHelper.is_supported_video?(upload.original_filename)
   152	
   153	          if optimized_video = OptimizedVideo.find_by(upload_id: upload.id)
   154	            # Ensure we maintain reference to optimized upload
   155	            UploadReference.ensure_exist!(
   156	              upload_ids: [optimized_video.optimized_upload.id],
   157	              target: @model,
   158	            )
   159	          end
   160	        end
   161	
   162	        # Update the chat message's cooked content if changes were made
   163	        if changes_made
   164	          new_cooked = @doc.to_html
   165	          @model.cooked = new_cooked
   166	          if !@model.save
   167	            Rails.logger.error(
   168	              "Failed to save chat message: #{@model.errors.full_messages.join(", ")}",
   169	            )
   170	          end
   171	        end
   172	      rescue => e
   173	        Rails.logger.error("Error in post_process_videos for chat message: #{e.message}")
   174	        Rails.logger.error(e.backtrace.join("\n"))
   175	      end
   176	    end
   177	  end
   178	end

    41	    def indexing_status(agent, uploads)
    42	      embeddings_table = DiscourseAi::Embeddings::Schema.for(self).table
    43	
    44	      results =
    45	        DB.query(
    46	          <<~SQL,
    47	        SELECT
    48	          uploads.id,
    49	          SUM(CASE WHEN (rdf.upload_id IS NOT NULL) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS total,
    50	          SUM(CASE WHEN (eft.rag_document_fragment_id IS NOT NULL) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as indexed,
    51	          SUM(CASE WHEN (rdf.upload_id IS NOT NULL AND eft.rag_document_fragment_id IS NULL) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as left
    52	        FROM uploads
    53	        LEFT OUTER JOIN rag_document_fragments rdf ON uploads.id = rdf.upload_id AND rdf.target_id = :target_id
    54	          AND rdf.target_type = :target_type
    55	        LEFT OUTER JOIN #{embeddings_table} eft ON rdf.id = eft.rag_document_fragment_id
    56	        WHERE uploads.id IN (:upload_ids)
    57	        GROUP BY uploads.id

    59	          target_id: agent.id,
    60	          target_type: agent.class.to_s,
    61	          upload_ids: uploads.map(&:id),
    62	        )

     3	module DiscourseAi
     4	  module Completions
     5	    class UploadEncoder
     6	      def self.encode(
     7	        upload_ids:,
     8	        max_pixels:,
     9	        allowed_kinds: [:image],
    10	        allowed_attachment_types: nil
    11	      )
    12	        uploads = []
    13	        allowed_attachment_types = normalize_attachment_types(allowed_attachment_types)
    14	        upload_ids.each do |upload_id|
    15	          upload = Upload.find(upload_id)
    16	          next if upload.blank?
    17	
    18	          extension = upload.extension&.downcase
    19	          kind = image_extension?(extension) ? :image : :document
    20	
    21	          next if allowed_kinds.exclude?(kind)
    22	
    23	          if kind == :document
    24	            mime_type =
    25	              MiniMime.lookup_by_filename(upload.original_filename)&.content_type ||
    26	                "application/octet-stream"
    27	
    28	            attachment_type = attachment_type_for(upload.extension, mime_type)
    29	            next if disallowed_attachment?(allowed_attachment_types, attachment_type)
    30	
    31	            payload = encode_document(upload, mime_type)
    32	            uploads << payload if payload
    33	            next
    34	          end
    35	
    36	          next if upload.width.to_i == 0 || upload.height.to_i == 0
    37	
    38	          desired_extension = upload.extension
    39	          desired_extension = "png" if upload.extension == "gif"
    40	          desired_extension = "png" if upload.extension == "webp"
    41	          desired_extension = "jpeg" if upload.extension == "jpg"
    42	
    43	          # this keeps it very simple format wise given everyone supports png and jpg
    44	          next if !%w[jpeg png].include?(desired_extension)
    45	
    46	          payload = encode_image(upload, desired_extension, max_pixels)
    47	          uploads << payload if payload
    48	        end
    49	        uploads
    50	      end
    51	
    52	      def self.attachment_type_for(extension, mime_type)
    53	        ext = extension.to_s.delete_prefix(".").downcase
    54	        mime = mime_type.to_s
    55	
    56	        return "pdf" if ext == "pdf" || mime.include?("pdf")
    57	        return "docx" if ext == "docx"
    58	        return "doc" if ext == "doc"
    59	        return "txt" if ext == "txt" || mime.include?("text/plain")
    60	        return "rtf" if ext == "rtf"
    61	        return "html" if %w[html htm].include?(ext) || mime.include?("html")
    62	        return "markdown" if %w[md markdown].include?(ext) || mime.include?("markdown")
    63	
    64	        "file"
    65	      end
    66

    72	
    73	          Array(types).map(&:downcase)
    74	        end
    75	
    76	        def disallowed_attachment?(allowed_types, attachment_type)
    77	          !allowed_types.nil? && !allowed_types.include?(attachment_type)
    78	        end

    64	        def invoke

    66	          custom_tools = self.class.available_custom_image_tools
    67	
    68	          if custom_tools.empty?

    70	            return(

    73	                error:
    74	                  "No image generation tools configured. Please configure an image generation tool via the admin UI to use this feature.",
    75	                give_up: true,
    76	              }
    77	            )
    78	          end
    79	
    80	          # Use the first available custom image tool
    81	          tool_class = custom_tools.first
    82	
    83	          # Map aspect ratio to size parameter if provided
    84	          size = aspect_ratio_to_size(aspect_ratio)
    85	
    86	          # Generate images for each prompt (up to 4)
    87	          selected_prompts = prompts.take(4)
    88	          progress = prompts.first

    95	            begin
    96	              # Create tool instance with parameters
    97	              tool_params = { prompt: prompt }
    98	              tool_params[:size] = size if size
    99	
   100	              tool_instance =
   101	                tool_class.new(tool_params, bot_user: bot_user, llm: llm, context: context)
   102	
   103	              # Invoke the tool
   104	              tool_instance.invoke { |_progress| }
   105	
   106	              # Extract the custom_raw which contains the generated image markdown
   107	              if tool_instance.custom_raw.present?

   109	                upload_match = tool_instance.custom_raw.match(%r{!\[.*?\]\((upload://[^)]+)\)})

   112	                  sha1 = Upload.sha1_from_short_url(short_url)

   116	                      prompt: prompt,
   117	                      upload: upload,
   118	                      seed: nil, # Custom tools don't provide seeds
   119	                    }
   120	                  end
   121	                end
   122	              end

   124	              Rails.logger.warn("Failed to generate image for prompt #{prompt}: #{e}")

   126	            end
   127	          end

   129	          if uploads.empty?
   130	            @chain_next_response = true
   131	            return(
   132	              {
   133	                prompts: prompts,
   134	                error:
   135	                  "Failed to generate images. #{errors.first || "Please check your image generation tool configuration."}",
   136	                give_up: true,
   137	              }
   138	            )
   139	          end

   142	
   143	          [grid]
   144	          #{
   145	            uploads

   154	            seeds: uploads.map { |item| item[:seed] },

   164	    def find_temp_file(s3_store, temp_path)
   165	      temp_object = s3_store.s3_helper.object(temp_path)
   166	      if !temp_object.exists?
   167	        Rails.logger.error(
   168	          "MediaConvert temp file not found at #{temp_path} for upload #{@upload.id}",
   169	        )
   170	        return nil
   171	      end
   172	
   173	      temp_object
   174	    end

   176	    def get_final_upload_path(new_sha1)
   177	      temp_upload = build_temp_upload_for_path_generation(new_sha1)
   178	      Discourse.store.get_path_for_upload(temp_upload)
   179	    end
   180	
   181	    def copy_file_to_final_location(s3_store, temp_path, final_path)
   182	      s3_helper = s3_store.s3_helper
   183	
   184	      destination_path = get_s3_path(final_path)
   185	
   186	      copy_options = s3_store.default_s3_options(secure: @upload.secure?)
   187	
   188	      begin
   189	        destination_path, etag = s3_helper.copy(temp_path, destination_path, options: copy_options)
   190	
   191	        destination_object = s3_helper.object(destination_path)
   192	        if !destination_object.exists?
   193	          Rails.logger.error(
   194	            "MediaConvert copy completed but destination file not found at #{destination_path} for upload #{@upload.id}",
   195	          )
   196	          [nil, nil]
   197	        end
   198	
   199	        # Log ACL settings for debugging
   200	        if copy_options[:acl].present?
   201	          Rails.logger.debug(
   202	            "MediaConvert copied file with ACL: #{copy_options[:acl]} (secure: #{@upload.secure?}) for upload #{@upload.id}",
   203	          )
   204	        end
   205	
   206	        [destination_path, etag]
   207	      rescue Aws::S3::Errors::NotFound => e
   208	        Rails.logger.error(
   209	          "MediaConvert copy failed - source or destination not found: #{e.message} (source: #{temp_path}, destination: #{destination_path}) for upload #{@upload.id}",
   210	        )
   211	        [nil, nil]
   212	      rescue Aws::S3::Errors::AccessDenied, Aws::S3::Errors::Forbidden => e
   213	        # Copy failed due to permissions - this is critical
   214	        Discourse.warn_exception(
   215	          e,
   216	          message: "MediaConvert copy failed due to permissions - ACL may not be applied",
   217	          env: {
   218	            upload_id: @upload.id,
   219	            temp_path: temp_path,
   220	            destination_path: destination_path,
   221	            secure: @upload.secure?,
   222	            copy_options: copy_options,
   223	          },
   224	        )
   225	        raise
   226	      rescue => e
   227	        Rails.logger.error(
   228	          "MediaConvert copy failed: #{e.class.name} - #{e.message} (source: #{temp_path}, destination: #{destination_path}) for upload #{@upload.id}",
   229	        )
   230	        raise
   231	      end
   232	    end
   233	
   234	    def update_file_acl(s3_store, destination_path)
   235	      begin
   236	        s3_store.update_file_access_control(destination_path, @upload.secure?)
   237	      rescue Aws::S3::Errors::NotFound => e
   238	        Rails.logger.error(
   239	          "MediaConvert file not found when updating access control at #{destination_path} for upload #{@upload.id}: #{e.message}",
   240	        )
   241	        raise
   242	      rescue Aws::S3::Errors::AccessDenied, Aws::S3::Errors::Forbidden => e
   243	        # ACL update failed due to permissions - this is critical as it will cause 403s
   244	        Discourse.warn_exception(
   245	          e,
   246	          message: "MediaConvert ACL update failed due to permissions - file may be inaccessible",
   247	          env: {
   248	            upload_id: @upload.id,
   249	            destination_path: destination_path,
   250	            secure: @upload.secure?,
   251	          },
   252	        )
   253	        raise
   254	      rescue Aws::S3::Errors::ServiceError => e
   255	        # Catch other AWS S3 errors that might prevent ACL from being set
   256	        Discourse.warn_exception(
   257	          e,
   258	          message: "MediaConvert ACL update failed with AWS error",
   259	          env: {
   260	            upload_id: @upload.id,
   261	            destination_path: destination_path,
   262	            secure: @upload.secure?,
   263	            error_code: e.code,
   264	          },
   265	        )
   266	        raise
   267	      end
   268	    end
   269	
   270	    def remove_temp_file(s3_store, temp_path)
   271	      s3_helper = s3_store.s3_helper
   272	      begin
   273	        s3_helper.remove(temp_path, false)
   274	      rescue => e
   275	        # Log but don't fail if deletion fails - file will be cleaned up later
   276	        Rails.logger.warn("Failed to delete temporary MediaConvert file #{temp_path}: #{e.message}")
   277	      end
   278	    end
   279	
   280	    def build_file_url(s3_store, destination_path)
   281	      # Ensure path has leading slash for URL
   282	      path_with_slash =
   283	        destination_path.start_with?("/") ? destination_path : "/#{destination_path}"
   284	      # Use s3_store.absolute_base_url which handles dualstack, region, and custom endpoints
   285	      "#{s3_store.absolute_base_url}#{path_with_slash}"
   286	    end
   287	
   288	    def get_output_path_for_record(final_path)
   289	      # Remove .mp4 extension for the record path
   290	      get_s3_path(final_path).sub(/\.mp4$/, "")
   291	    end
   292	
   293	    def get_s3_path(path)
   294	      return path if path.blank?
   295	
   296	      if Rails.configuration.multisite
   297	        multisite_prefix = build_multisite_path
   298	        # Prevent double-prepending if path already includes the multisite prefix
   299	        return path if path.start_with?(multisite_prefix)
   300	        File.join(multisite_prefix, path)
   301	      else
   302	        path
   303	      end
   304	    end
   305	
   306	    def build_multisite_path
   307	      path = File.join("uploads", RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.current_db)
   308	      "#{path}/"
   309	    end
   310	
   311	    def build_temp_upload_for_path_generation(new_sha1)
   312	      # Create a temporary upload object to leverage FileStore::BaseStore path generation
   313	      # This object is only used for path generation and won't be saved to the database
   314	      Upload.new(
   315	        id: @upload.id, # Use the same ID to get the same depth calculation
   316	        sha1: new_sha1,
   317	        extension: "mp4",
   318	      )
   319	    end
   320	
   321	    def mediaconvert_client
   322	      @mediaconvert_client ||= build_client
   323	    end
   324	
   325	    def build_client
   326	      # For some reason the endpoint is not visible in the aws console UI so we need to get it from the API
   327	      if SiteSetting.mediaconvert_endpoint.blank?
   328	        client = create_basic_client
   329	        resp = client.describe_endpoints
   330	        SiteSetting.mediaconvert_endpoint = resp.endpoints[0].url
   331	      end
   332	
   333	      # Validate that we have an endpoint before proceeding
   334	      if SiteSetting.mediaconvert_endpoint.blank?
   335	        error_msg = "MediaConvert endpoint is required but could not be discovered"
   336	        Discourse.warn_exception(
   337	          StandardError.new(error_msg),
   338	          message: error_msg,
   339	          env: {
   340	            upload_id: @upload.id,
   341	          },
   342	        )
   343	        raise StandardError, error_msg
   344	      end
   345	
   346	      create_basic_client(endpoint: SiteSetting.mediaconvert_endpoint)
   347	    end
   348	
   349	    def create_basic_client(endpoint: nil)
   350	      client_options = { region: SiteSetting.s3_region }
   351	      client_options[:endpoint] = endpoint if endpoint.present?
   352	      creds = S3Helper.s3_credentials(SiteSetting)
   353	      client_options[:credentials] = creds if creds
   354	
   355	      Aws::MediaConvert::Client.new(client_options)
   356	    end
   357	
   358	    def s3_upload_bucket
   359	      self.class.s3_upload_bucket
   360	    end
   361	
   362	    def self.s3_upload_bucket
   363	      # MediaConvert needs just the bucket name, not the folder path
   364	      # If s3_upload_bucket is "bucket-name/folder", we need just "bucket-name"
   365	      bucket_name, _folder_path =
   366	        S3Helper.get_bucket_and_folder_path(SiteSetting.Upload.s3_upload_bucket)
   367	      bucket_name
   368	    end
   369	
   370	    def build_conversion_settings(input_path, output_path)
   371	      self.class.build_conversion_settings(input_path, output_path)
   372	    end
   373	
   374	    def self.build_conversion_settings(input_path, temp_output_filename)
   375	      # temp_output_filename is just the filename without extension (e.g., "new_sha1")
   376	      # MediaConvert will automatically add .mp4 extension based on container type
   377	      # We write it to the subdirectory as a temporary location
   378	      subdirectory = SiteSetting.mediaconvert_output_subdirectory
   379	      destination_path = File.join(subdirectory, temp_output_filename)

     4	module VideoConversion
     5	  class AwsMediaConvertAdapter < BaseAdapter
     6	    ADAPTER_NAME = "aws_mediaconvert"
     7	
     8	    def convert
     9	      return false if !valid_settings?
    10	
    11	      begin
    12	        new_sha1 = SecureRandom.hex(20)
    13	
    14	        # Extract the path from the URL
    15	        # The URL format is: //bucket.s3.dualstack.region.amazonaws.com/path/to/file
    16	        # or: //bucket.s3.region.amazonaws.com/path/to/file
    17	        url = @upload.url.sub(%r{^//}, "") # Remove leading //
    18	
    19	        # Split on the first / to separate the domain from the path
    20	        domain, path = url.split("/", 2)
    21	
    22	        # Verify the domain contains our bucket
    23	        if !domain&.include?(s3_upload_bucket)
    24	          raise Discourse::InvalidParameters.new(
    25	                  "Upload URL domain for upload ID #{@upload.id} does not contain expected bucket name: #{s3_upload_bucket}",
    26	                )
    27	        end
    28	
    29	        input_path = "s3://#{s3_upload_bucket}/#{path}"
    30	
    31	        settings = build_conversion_settings(input_path, new_sha1)
    32	
    33	        begin
    34	          response =
    35	            mediaconvert_client.create_job(
    36	              role: SiteSetting.mediaconvert_role_arn,
    37	              settings: settings,
    38	              status_update_interval: "SECONDS_10",
    39	              user_metadata: {
    40	                "upload_id" => @upload.id.to_s,
    41	                "new_sha1" => new_sha1,
    42	              },
    43	            )
    44	
    45	          # Enqueue status check job
    46	          Jobs.enqueue_in(
    47	            30.seconds,
    48	            :check_video_conversion_status,
    49	            upload_id: @upload.id,
    50	            job_id: response.job.id,
    51	            new_sha1: new_sha1,
    52	            original_filename: @upload.original_filename,
    53	            user_id: @upload.user_id,
    54	            adapter_type: "aws_mediaconvert",
    55	          )
    56
